acrobat.name: Acrobat
description: ## Acrobat
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/132›

Agility and daring are both excellent rogue traits, and their confluence can create spectacular feats of acrobatics. Whether they are daring thieves, infiltrating assassins, or intrepid spies, proper training in acrobatics is a valuable boon for rogues.

::ab[Expert Acrobat (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="An acrobat does not suffer any armor check penalties on Acrobatics, Climb, Fly, Sleight of Hand, or Stealth skill checks while wearing light armor. When she is not wearing armor, she gains a +2 competency bonus on Acrobatics and Fly skill checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Second Chance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=roll ability="An acrobat can reroll any Acrobatics, Climb, or Fly skill check she has just made. This reroll is made at a -5 penalty. She must take the second result, even if it is worse. An acrobat can use this ability only once on any given skill check." useInc=rogue~3 useF="3~1" replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Expert leaper~fast getaway~ledge walker~nimble climber~peerless maneuver~rogue crawl~stand up"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Defensive roll~fast tumble~improved evasion"}

bandit.name: Bandit
description: ## Bandit
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/71›

Bandits, brigands, and highwaymen hone their craft along the roadsides and byways of the world, where they leap from the shadows to plunder the spoils of passing travelers. Bandits tend to have a variety of skills; sometimes these skills are similar to a ranger's, but a bandit's tactics always deal with surprise followed by intimidation, with the clear threat of naked force if intimidation does not do the trick. But not all bandits are ruthless thugs. There are those who become outlaws in opposition to tyrants or similar oppressors. These bandits target the status quo and distribute their spoils back into the needy hands of the peasantry.

::ab[Ambush (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="A bandit becomes fully practiced in the art of ambushing." ability="When she acts in the surprise round, she can take a move action, standard action, and swift action during the surprise round, not just a move or standard action." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Fearsome Strike (Ex)]{l=8 icon=lower flavor="A bandit can terrify an opponent with a single hit." useMod=Charisma useM=1 ability="When a bandit confirms a critical hit and deals sneak attack damage to an opponent, she can choose to make the opponent ‹misc/frightened› for a number of rounds equal to her Charisma modifier (minimum 1). She cannot use this ability in conjunction with a critical feat." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Assault leader~combat trick~cunning trigger~snap shot~strong impression~surprise attack~terrain mastery~underhanded~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~knock-out blow~opportunist"}

bekyar_kidnapper.name: Bekyar Kidnapper
description: ## Bekyar Kidnapper
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/36›

One of the many ethnicities found in the Mwangi Expanse, the Bekyar people are slavers and demon worshipers. A Bekyar kidnapper is not only adept at hiding herself in thick jungles and quickly subduing her prey, but also at spreading rumors of hidden ruins, buried treasure, and other such valuables that might lure an unwary foreigner into her traps.

::ab[Clean Capture (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A Bekyar kidnapper reduces the penalty to her combat maneuver check to tie up a pinned or otherwise restrained target by an amount equal to 1/2 her rogue level." order=pa ability="She can take the feats ‹feat/Improved Grapple› and ‹feat/Greater Grapple› as rogue talents. She can ignore the ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› requirement when selecting these feats as rogue talents, but must meet all other prerequisites." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Abductor (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def passive="A Bekyar kidnapper gains a bonus on combat maneuver checks to grapple a foe. @B-3f-rogue. In addition, the Bekyar kidnapper adds this bonus to her combat maneuver bonus when a foe tries to grapple her or when a grappled target attempts to break free of her grapple." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~camouflage~combat trick~rope master~survivalist~terrain mastery"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~hide in plain sight~knock-out blow~rumormonger~weapon snatcher"}

bellflower_irrigator.name: Bellflower Irrigator
description: ## Bellflower Irrigator
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/43›

Bellflower irrigators take on tasks that many other agents of the ‹rule/Bellflower Network› find distasteful. Rather than focusing on saving slaves, Bellflower irrigators seek to defeat slavery by aiming to eliminate the source. They gauge the precise moment to strike from the shadows and annihilate slave traders and masters alike. Bellflower irrigators realize that for every slave released today, another will be shackled tomorrow - until all the masters are dead. This cynicism leads Bellflower irrigators to target anyone who owns or sells slaves on Golarion, especially cruel masters within Cheliax.

Though most Bellflower irrigators are ‹race/halfling«s›, this branch of the Network is more diverse than many of the others - anyone with a hatred of slavery and a talent for stealth and martial combat is free to join. Bellflower irrigators employ combat methods that many find questionable, such as the use of poison and calculated strikes meant to kill rather than incapacitate. The Bellflower Network does not knowingly allow evil members, but very few Bellflower irrigators are good - most are chaotic neutral in alignment, as their methods of solving the problem of slavery often force them into moral and ethical gray areas. A few Bellflower irrigators are in fact evil, but these rarities do their best to hide their personal lack of morals and addiction to cruelty in order to maintain their positions and connection to the Bellflowers, for as evil as they may be, they share a hatred of slavery with their more altruistic kin. An evil Bellflower irrigator who is found out is typically exiled from the group, yet most continue to fight slavery even after they are sent away in this way.

::ab[Bellflower Crop (Ex)]{icon=aura standard="A Bellflower irrigator can designate a number of allies equal to 3 + his Charisma modifier as part of his *Bellflower crop.* Members of a *Bellflower crop* must remain within 30 feet of the Bellflower irrigator to gain any benefits from abilities that affect a *Bellflower crop,* regardless of the ability's source (whether it's a Bellflower harvester, irrigator, or tiller). If the allies leave this range, the Bellflower irrigator must designate them again to include them in his crop."}
::ab[Poison Use (Ex)]{icon=def ability="A Bellflower irrigator is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying poison to a weapon." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Grafting (Ex)]{l=4 icon=lower passive="A Bellflower irrigator is considered to be ‹rule/flanking› an opponent if at least two members of his *Bellflower crop* are threatening that opponent, regardless of the irrigator's actual positioning." replace="Uncanny dodge~rogue talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Irrigation (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="If a Bellflower irrigator studies his target for 3 rounds (spending a @HLstandard_action each round to do so) and then makes a sneak attack with a melee weapon that successfully deals damage, the attack has the additional effect of possibly either ‹rule/paralyz«ing<ed› or killing the target (Bellflower irrigator's choice). The death attack fails if the target detects the Bellflower irrigator or recognizes him as an enemy. If the victim of the attack fails a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the Bellflower irrigator's class level + the Bellflower irrigator's Intelligence modifier) against the kill effect, she dies. If the saving throw fails against the paralysis effect, the victim is rendered helpless and unable to act for 1d6 rounds plus 1 round per 2 rogue levels that the Bellflower irrigator has. If the victim's saving throw succeeds, the attack is just a normal sneak attack. Once the Bellflower irrigator has completed the 3 rounds of study, he must make the irrigation attempt within the next 3 rounds." useInc=rogue~4~8 useF="8~1~4" replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

burglar.name: Burglar
description: ## Burglar
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/132›

Adept at infiltration, trap removal, and lock picking, burglars prey on the homes of the wealthy and forgotten tombs alike. Such riches are guarded with vicious traps and hazards, but the devious mind of the burglar allows her to find such dangers and avoid them.

::ab[Careful Disarm (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="Whenever a burglar attempts to disarm a trap using Disable Device, she does not spring the trap unless she fails by 10 or more. If she does set off a trap she was attempting to disarm, she adds double her *trap sense* bonus to avoid the trap." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Distraction (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def ability="Whenever a burglar is detected while using Stealth, she can immediately attempt a Bluff skill check opposed by the Sense Motive skill of the creature that spotted her. If this check succeeds, the target assumes that the noise was something innocent and disregards the detection. This only functions if the creature cannot see the rogue. This ability can only be used once during a given Stealth attempt. If the same creature detects the rogue's presence again, the ability has no effect." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Fast picks~fast stealth~nimble climber~quick disable~trap spotter"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Knock-out blow~skill mastery~thoughtful reexamining"}

carnivalist.name: Carnivalist
description: ## Carnivalist
‹SOURCE Animal Archive/6›
Carnivalists train their miniature minions to perform acts of larceny and often hide their true talents behind theatrical sideshows.
::cskill{gain=ha}

::ab[Familiar]{l=1 icon=power ability="A carnivalist gains a ‹sidekick/familiar› as a wizard of equal level. Levels in a class that grants a familiar stack with carnivalist levels for determining the familiar's abilities."}

::ab[Pet Performance (Ex)]{head icon=power ability="A carnivalist gains use of several ‹ability/bardic performances›, making Handle Animal checks in place of any Perform checks." imp2="She gains the ‹performance/distraction› bardic performance." imp4="She gains the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance." imp6="She gains use of the performance below." replace="Rogue talents gained at 2nd, 4th, and 6th level"}

::ab[Trained Legerdemain (Ex)]{next l=6 icon=boost ability="A carnivalist can command a familiar or pet within 30 feet to make a Disable Device or Sleight of Hand check as if trained in the skill, using its own skill ranks (if any), Dexterity modifier, and equipment, with a competence bonus equal to 1/2 her rogue level. The check requires the normal amount of time to complete and the DC increases by 5. The carnivalist must maintain this pet performance each round her familiar or pet attempts a Disable Device or Sleight of Hand check." special="The pet performance ability otherwise functions identically to bardic performance. Levels in a class with the *distraction* or *fascinate* bardic performances stack with carnivalist levels for the purpose of determining bonuses granted by these performances."}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{icon=down-power l2="A carnivalist gains this ability starting at 2nd level. The sneak attack damage dealt is 1d6 points. A carnivalist's familiar can also deal sneak attack damage as appropriate to foes as long as it is within 30 feet of her (though only Small and larger creatures may flank enemies, as usual)." incrementPlain="The sneak attack damage~2~4" incrementEnd="d6 points." alter="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Animal Trainer (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-power ability="A carnivalist receives a bonus equal to 1/2 her rogue level whenever she uses Handle Animal on a Tiny or Small animal. In addition, she can increase the DC by 5 to reduce the time needed to teach an animal a new trick or train an animal for a general purpose to 1 day for every week normally required. She can also train more than one animal at once, although each animal after the first adds 2 to the DC." replace="Trap sense"}

cat_burglar.name: Cat Burglar
description: ## Cat Burglar
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/92›

Gifted with finesse and stealth, catfolk make excellent burglars. Cat burglars are masters of breaking and entering, using their feline grace to make it seem as though no crime was ever committed in the first place. Few locks can withstand skilled cat burglars, and such nimble rogues are capable of bypassing traps without activating them and enabling associates to do the same. A cat burglar has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Catfolk}

::ab[Phantom Presence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def-boost flavor="A cat burglar masters stealthy movement and leaves no trace of her passing in dungeons and cities." ability="While in dungeon and urban environments, she leaves no trail and cannot be tracked, though she can choose to leave behind a trail if she so desires. Furthermore, she can always choose to take 10 when making a Stealth check." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Trap Saboteur (Su)]{l=8 icon=power-boost flavor="A cat burglar becomes a master of avoiding and manipulating traps and locks." ability="She can attempt to open a lock as a @HLstandard_action and takes 1/2 the normal amount of time to disable traps (minimum 1 round). When she has bypassed a trap without disarming it, she can also choose to suppress its trigger for up to 1 minute. If she does, she can also choose to end this suppression prematurely as a @HLfree_action." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
@HL[These rogue talents complement this archetype:] ‹talent/Fast picks›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/quick disable›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›@FN1
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~fast tumble~hide in plain sight"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The original text also recommended *convincing fakes* and *dodge trap.* However, these talents do not exist - the former may refer to the Deception Talent ‹talent/sczarni smuggler›, though.]{from=1}

chameleon.name: Chameleon
description: ## Chameleon
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/71›

An absolute master of disguise, a chameleon effortlessly blends into any environment. Whether disappearing into crowded city streets, vanishing into desert sands, or slipping into the darkness of subterranean tunnels, the chameleon relies upon her ability to become part of her surroundings.

::ab[Misdirection (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="A chameleon begins her career knowing that the secret to disappearing lies in deceiving the senses of her observers." ability="Every day she gains a pool of stealth points equal to her ranks in Bluff. These points refresh at the start of each day. Before making a Stealth check, she can choose to put stealth points into the roll, gaining a bonus on Stealth checks equal to the number of stealth points she puts into the roll." special="If she gains a bonus on Bluff checks because of a feat (such as ‹feat/Skill Focus› &#91&Bluff&#93&), she adds a number of points to her stealth pool equal to the bonus the feat grants." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Effortless Sneak (Sp)]{l=3 icon=power ability="The chameleon chooses a single terrain from the ranger's ‹ability/favored terrain› class feature. While she is within that terrain, she can take 10 on any Stealth check she can make within that terrain." repeat="She chooses a new type of terrain and gains this ability with the newly picked terrain.~6~3" replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~fast stealth~quick disguise~terrain mastery"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~master of disguise"}

charlatan.name: Charlatan
description: ## Charlatan
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/72›

A charlatan is a master of lies and deception. Whether creating simple cons or elaborately woven hoaxes capable of swaying the masses and those in positions of power, a charlatan is often a purveyor of snake-oil, forgeries, and rumormongering.

::ab[Natural Born Liar (Ex)]{l=1 icon=lower ability="When a charlatan successfully deceives a creature with a Bluff, that creature takes a -2 penalty on the charlatan's Bluff checks for the next 24 hours. This ability does not stack with itself." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Grand Hoax (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power flavor="The charlatan begins to master the art of the *grand hoax* and learns to create deceptions designed to bestow harm upon her foes." ability="She gains the ‹talent/rumormonger› advanced talent, even though she is not yet 10th level." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~coax information~convincing lie~honeyed words~underhanded"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hard minded~skill mastery~unwitting ally"}

consigliere.name: Consigliere
description: ## Consigliere
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/44›

Consiglieres act as trusted mediators and administrators for crime families and various criminal organizations, and many serve as the right hand to the leaders of such groups. Other consiglieres prefer to work as independent operators and sell their advice and expertise to the highest bidder. Though some consiglieres insulate themselves from violence and criminal activities, many enjoy getting their hands dirty leading strike teams or gangs of hardened thugs.
::cskill{gain=kno lose=kd}

::ab[Convincing Attitude (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A consigliere gains ‹feat/Persuasive› as a bonus feat." l2="He gains the ‹talent/charmer› rogue talent (or the ‹talent/certainty› &#91&Diplomacy&#93& rogue talent if he is an unchained rogue)." l3="If a consigliere attempts a Diplomacy check and fails to influence a creature's attitude by 5 or more, the creature's attitude remains unchanged instead of decreasing by one step." replace=Trapfinding~evasion}

::ab[Combat Advisor (Ex)]{icon=boost l4="Whenever a consigliere misses with a melee attack against an opponent, he can designate an ally within 30 feet of the target to receive a +1 insight bonus on her next attack roll against that opponent before the consigliere's next turn." l8="The insight bonus increases to +2." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Bonus Feats (Ex)]{icon=power l4="A consigliere can choose to gain a single ‹main/teamwork feat› instead of a single rogue talent. (This substitution can only be used once, at either 4th, 6th, or 8th level.)" l12="The consigliere can choose to gain teamwork feats instead of rogue talents as many times as she chooses." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Field Boss (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power standard="A consigliere can grant a teamwork feat to all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear her. This teamwork feat must be one the consigliere knows. Allies retain the use of this feat for 3 rounds plus 1 round for every 2 rogue levels the consigliere has. Allies do not need to meet the prerequisites of this teamwork feat." useInc=rogue~4~10 useF="10~1~4" replace="Rogue talent normally gained at 10th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent all="assault leader~black market connections~shades of gray~coax information~honeyed words~certainty|Bluff; unchained rogue only"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hard minded~opportunist~redirect attack~rumormonger~skill mastery~slippery mind~unwitting ally"}

construct_saboteur.name: Construct Saboteur
description: ## Construct Saboteur
‹SOURCE Construct Handbook/12›
The construct saboteur harnesses arcane energy to combat the golems and eldritch guardians that prowl the depths of ancient ruins and forgotten tombs.
::cskill{gain=ka~ke lose=kd~kl}

::ab[Arcane Strike (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A construct saboteur gains ‹feat/Arcane Strike› as a bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites. She uses her construct saboteur class level in place of her caster level for the purposes of the feat's effects." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Arcane Sabotage (Su)]{head l=2 icon=power flavor="A construct saboteur can user her arcane prowess to hinder constructs." ability="She gains one sabotage ability (listed below). Whenever she uses Arcane Strike to attack a construct, she can activate one of her sabotage abilities that she has gained." imp4="From here on out, she can select any sabotage ability in place of a rogue talent." special="The DC for any sabotage ability that requires a saving throw is 10 + half the construct saboteur's level + the construct saboteur's Intelligence modifier." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Diminish Senses]{next icon=lower ability="A jolt of energy overwhelms the construct's senses. The construct is ‹misc/blinded› for 1 round. A construct saboteur can forgo dealing sneak attack damage to extend the duration of the blindness by 1 round for every 2d6 points of sneak attack damage forgone in this way. A construct can negate this effect with a successful Will save."}

::ab[Magic Vulnerability]{next icon=lower ability="A field of energy enshrouds the construct, disabling its immunity to magic for 1 round. The construct instead gains spell resistance equal to 15 + its CR. A construct saboteur can forgo dealing sneak attack damage to reduce this spell resistance. The construct's spell resistance is reduced by 1 for every 1d6 points of sneak attack damage forgone in this way. A construct can negate this effect with a successful Will save."}

::ab[Overwhelming Jolt]{next icon=lower ability="A sudden burst of power moves over the surface of the construct, giving it the ‹misc/staggered› condition for 1 round. A construct can negate this effect with a successful Will save."}

::ab[Slowing Pulse]{next icon=lower ability="A surge of slowing energy ripples through the construct, reducing its speed by 5 feet for a number of rounds equal to the construct saboteur's Intelligence modifier. A construct saboteur can forgo dealing sneak attack damage to reduce the construct's speed by an additional 5 feet for every 1d6 points of sneak attack damage forgone in this way. This ability cannot lower a construct's movement speed below 5 feet. A construct can reduce the duration of this effect to 1 round with a successful Reflex save."}

::ab[Dismantling Strikes (Ex)]{icon=boost l3="A construct saboteur ignores the first 2 points of DR or hardness when attacking constructs." incrementPlain="The amount of DR ignored~3~3~4~2" replace="Trap sense"}

counterfeit_mage.name: Counterfeit Mage
description: ## Counterfeit Mage
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/109›

Charlatans and stage magicians use sleight of hand to fake magic. A counterfeit mage goes a step further, parroting the motions and activation phrases used by arcane casters to activate wands or other magical accoutrements. While counterfeit mages rarely fool a real wizard, their command of the arcane is enough to convince most lay people.

::ab[Magical Expertise (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-power order=pa passive="A counterfeit mage adds 1/2 his level to Disable Device checks to disarm magical traps, Perception checks to find magical traps, and Use Magic Device checks to activate scrolls and wands." ability="A counterfeit mage can use Disable Device to disarm magic traps." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Signature Wand (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A counterfeit mage can spend 1 hour practicing with a wand to designate it as his signature wand. He can draw that wand as a @HLfree_action, and can activate it without having to succeed at a Use Magic Device check. He can change his signature wand once per day." replace="Rogue talent gained at 4th level"}
::ab[Wand Adept (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost ability="A counterfeit mage can use his Dexterity modifier in place of his Charisma modifier when attempting Use Magic Device checks to activate wands."}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deft palm~esoteric scholar~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic~trap spotter"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Dispelling attack~familiar~slippery mind"}

cutpurse.name: Cutpurse
description: ## Cutpurse
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/132›

Everyone who lives in the city understands that a wealthy man stays wealthy by guarding his purse while wandering the streets and markets. Cutpurses are often trained by guilds to collect the guild's tax from local businesses on a daily basis, whether through intimidation or pickpocketing. Some find themselves taking up the mantle of adventurer, and their talents are generally appreciated in this role, but the cutpurse is still the first person her companions look to when an item goes missing.

::ab[Measure the Mark (Ex)]{icon=power ability="When a cutpurse makes a Sleight of Hand check to take something from a creature, the target makes its Perception check before the rogue makes her Sleight of Hand check, and the rogue knows the Perception check result. She can decide whether or not to make the check based on the results of the target's Perception check. If the rogue elects not to make the check, she can make a Bluff check, opposed by the target's Sense Motive, to prevent the target from noticing the attempt." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Stab and Grab (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power fullround="A cutpurse can make an attack and also make a Sleight of Hand check to steal something from the target of the attack. If the attack deals sneak attack damage, the rogue can use Sleight of Hand to take an item from the creature during combat; otherwise this ability can only be used in a surprise round before the target has acted. If the attack is successful, the target takes a -5 penalty on the Perception check to notice the theft." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Combat swipe~fast fingers~fast getaway~hard to fool~minor magic~slow reactions~surprise attack"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~fast tumble~skill mastery"}

dark_lurker.name: Dark Lurker
description: ## Dark Lurker
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/26›

Dark lurkers use darkness as an ally. They are widely feared in many major metropolises, spoken of as "living shadows" by guards of Magnimar who patrol under the Irespan and called "the hungry night" by citizens of Absalom who mistake them for fetchling assassins.

::ab[Blades from the Shadows (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A dark lurker gains ‹feat/Shadow Strike› as a bonus feat. (A character using the unchained rogue class instead gains the ability to make attacks of opportunity against foes with ‹rule/cover›, though not foes with improved cover or total cover)." l6="The dark lurker can deal ‹misc/precision damage› to targets with ‹rule/total »concealment› (though any miss chance applies normally, and may prevent an attack from hitting in the first place)." special="The dark lurker can take ‹ability/evasion› as a rogue talent." replace="Evasion"}
::ab[Blind-Fight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A dark lurker gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}
::ab[Improved Blind-Fight (Ex)]{icon=power l8="A dark lurker gains ‹feat/Improved Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." replace="Rogue talent gained at 8th level"}
::ab[Greater Blind-Fight (Ex)]{icon=power l14="A dark lurker gains ‹feat/Greater Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." replace="Rogue talent gained at 14th level"}
::ab[Instinctual Sense (Ex)]{icon=power l20="A dark lurker gains ‹umr/blindsight› with a range of 30 feet." replace="Rogue talent gained at 20th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Befuddling strike~fast getaway~fast stealth~sniper's eye~surprise attack"}
@HL[This advanced rogue talent complements this archetype:] ‹talent/Opportunist›.

deadly_courtesan.name: Deadly Courtesan
description: ## Deadly Courtesan
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/208›

Skilled at manipulation and diversion, the deadly courtesan builds up those around her and periodically takes them down. She can be a spy, entertainer, assassin, bodyguard, or just an intimate to someone who needs it most. A deadly courtesan has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Vishkanya}
::cskill{gain=kh~kno lose=kd}

::ab[Bardic Performance (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A deadly courtesan gains the ‹ability/bardic performance› ability and the ‹performance/fascinate› bardic performance. Her fascinate DC is 10 + 1/2 her rogue level + her Charisma modifier." usage="A number of rounds/day equal to her rogue level - 1 + her Charisma modifier" special="If the courtesan also has bard levels, she may use these rounds for either class's *fascinate* bardic performance, and her bard and rogue levels stack for determining her fascinate DC." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Inspire Competence (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A deadly courtesan can use her bardic performance to ‹performance/inspire competence›. The bonus begins at +2." increment="The bonus~3~6~3" replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Performance Strike (Sp)]{l=8 icon=boost swift="A deadly courtesan may expend rounds of bardic performance to gain a morale bonus on one attack roll. The amount of the bonus is equal to the number of bardic performance rounds expended (maximum bonus equal to 1/2 her deadly courtesan level)." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Charmer~coax information~convincing lie~deft palm~fast fingers~finesse rogue~honeyed words~stand up"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Master of disguise~rumormonger~slippery mind~unwitting ally"}

desert_raider.name: Desert Raider
description: ## Desert Raider
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/76›

Desert raiders constantly threaten caravans that cross vast, desolate expanses. Some desert raiders are tribespeople who raid to supplement their hardscrabble existence, while others are career thieves who take to the desert to avoid the noose. To survive, a desert raider must learn the secrets of the deserts and become inured to their dangers. Though most often associated with hot deserts, desert raiders can also operate in tundra, where sunlight on snow can blind as easily as on sand.
::cskill{gain=kg~sur lose=kd~swim}

::ab[Desert Tracker (Ex)]{icon=def-boost passive="A desert raider is immune to the ‹misc/dazzled› condition. A desert raider adds half her level (minimum 1) as a bonus on Survival skill checks to follow or identify tracks in desert terrain." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Sun at Your Back (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A desert raider can use the reflected light of the blinding sun to conceal her presence." ability="A desert raider in bright light can attempt Stealth checks without cover or concealment but takes a -5 penalty on her check. The desert raider cannot use this ability to hide from creatures immune to the ‹misc/blinded› or dazzled conditions." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Light Step (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def-boost passive="The DC of Survival checks to track the desert raider increase by 2 + ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level. In addition, she gains a bonus on Perception checks to avoid being surprised by a foe. This bonus on is equal to one-third of her rogue level." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Fast stealth~getaway artist~ledge walker~rogue crawl~stand up~surprise attack"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~stealthy sniper"}

discretion_specialist.name: Discretion Specialist
description: ## Discretion Specialist
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/25›

Often brought in to handle messy situations, discretion specialists deal with bodies, inconvenient witnesses, and other loose ends. Many organizations employ them to cover up activities that might be unpopular - should they become known to the general populace.
This archetype is appropriate for rogues and unchained rogues.
::ab[Fast Talker (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A discretion specialist adds half her rogue level (minimum +1) as a bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Obfuscation (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="A discretion specialist is highly skilled at convincing others not to trust their own senses." fullround="The discretion specialist can convince one living creature within 30 feet that up to 5 minutes of its memories from the past hour are unreliable. If the target fails a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the discretion specialist's rogue level + her Intelligence modifier), the target is convinced that those memories didn't happen or that it's confusing the memories with a dream (or is otherwise hazy as to the memories' reality). Each hour after the discretion specialist uses this ability, the target can attempt a new Will saving throw to realize that its relevant memories have been tampered with, although not necessarily that the discretion specialist was the originator of the tampering." useInc=rogue~5~+ useF="3~1~5~~5" incrementPlain="The discretion specialist can obfuscate memories from up~3~3" incrementDesc=to incrementEnd=" hours in the past." replace="Trap sense (for a core rogue)~danger sense (for an unchained rogue)"}

::ab[Evidence Disposal (Sp)]{flavor="A discretion specialist can cover up a corpse's cause of a death - or dispose of it entirely." icon=magic l4="The discretion specialist gains the ability to cast ‹spell/dress corpse› as a spell-like ability, using her rogue level as her caster level. She can use this ability a number of times per day equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of her rogue level." l12="She can spend two uses of this ability to disintegrate a corpse entirely as a @HLfull-round_action, leaving behind only a trace of fine dust (a disintegrated creature's equipment is unaffected). The target cannot be an undead creature. A corpse disintegrated this way cannot be brought back to life except by ‹spell/resurrection› or more powerful magic, including ‹spell/miracle› or ‹spell/wish›." replace="Rogue talents gained at 4th and 12th levels"}

::ab[No Loose Ends (Ex)]{icon=lower flavor="The discretion specialist can prevent opponents from escaping." l4="Opponents damaged by the discretion specialist's sneak attack are unable to take the ‹rule/withdraw› action for 1 round, though they can still move as normal." l8="A creature damaged by the discretion specialist's sneak attack is hampered in casting spells of the teleportation subschool (such as ‹spell/dimension door›) as well as using methods of magical transport such as ‹spell/shadow walk›. A target attempting to cast such a spell before the end of the discretion specialist's next turn must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (in addition to any concentration checks required for casting while threatened or other circumstances) or lose the spell. The DC of this check is equal to 10 + half the discretion specialist's rogue level + her Intelligence modifier." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~charmer~convincing lie~honeyed words~quick disguise"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~master of disguise"}

dreamthief.name: Dreamthief
description: ## Dreamthief
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/12›

The dreamthief is master of both waking and dreaming worlds. Each dreamthief bears a focused fragment of emotion, known as a dreamshard, within her soul. Some are born with such fragments, while others gain their dreamshards during traumatic moments, especially those involving nightmares, attacks by ethereal beings, or incidents that take place in the Dimension of Dreams. Whether a dreamshard is shaped from the dreamer's aggregated emotions or manifested from a repressed aspect of herself, this metaphysical crystal grants a dreamthief supernatural powers, which she supplements with skill and grace.

Dreamthieves' powers grant them enhanced access to ‹rule/dimension_of_>dreams«capes›. Using the unique power of her dreamshard, a knowledgeable dreamthief can crystallize and steal dreamers' deepest emotions. Such theft can alter dreamers in profound ways, whether for better or worse.
::cskill{gain=ka~kp}

::ab[Dreamshard Focus (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-magic choice="A dreamthief selects an ‹ability/emotional focus› from among those available to a spiritualist's phantom. She gains the abilities granted by that emotional focus (with the exception of the focus determining her ‹misc/good saving throws› - she retains the typical rogue saves). For each of the two skills associated with that emotional focus, a dreamthief gains ranks in that skill equal to her rogue level; this does not allow her ranks in these skills to exceed her Hit Dice." passive="A dreamthief is considered to be both a phantom and a spiritualist for the purpose of emotional focus abilities whose effect references both a phantom and a spiritualist, such as *dutiful strike,* and she treats her rogue level as both her spiritualist level and her phantom Hit Dice when determining abilities and save DCs." ability="Once per round when a dreamthief makes a melee attack, she can declare that attack to be a *dreamstrike.* This does not require an action, but a dreamthief must declare a *dreamstrike* before making the attack roll. A *dreamstrike* is treated as a slam attack for the purpose of emotional focus abilities that apply to a phantom's slam attacks, such as *miserable strike.*" imp20="Any melee attack a dreamthief makes automatically gains this benefit." order=cpa replace="Sneak attack~master strike"}
::ab[Lucid Dreamer (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A dreamthief can control her dreams, and gains ‹feat/Lucid Dreamer› as a bonus feat." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Soothe Dreaming (Su)]{l=4 icon=aid ability="A dreamthief can steal negative emotions, freeing allies from harmful conditions. This acts as the ‹class/mesmerist«'s› *touch treatment* ability, treating her rogue level as her mesmerist level." useInc=rogue~3 useF="4~1~3~~6" replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Dream Infiltrator (Sp)]{head icon=aid flavor="A dreamthief can enter dreams, whether to learn secrets or meddle more directly." l8="A dreamthief can cast ‹spell/dream scan› once per day as a spell-like ability." l12="Once per day she can cast either *dream scan* or ‹spell/dream travel› as a spell-like ability." special="The caster level of these spell-like abilities is equal to her rogue level." replace="Improved uncanny dodge~rogue talent gained at 12th level"}

::ab[Touch Treatment (Su)]{flavor="Text from the mesmerist ability." next icon=aid l3="You can help allies shake off harmful conditions - especially those that affect their minds - by channeling psychic energy through your healing hands. You must be able to touch your target. You can remove the *fascinated* or *shaken* condition from your target each time you use this ability. This is the &quot&minor&quot& ability." imp6="You can now also remove the *confused, dazed, frightened,* and *sickened* conditions. This is the &quot&moderate&quot& ability." imp10="You can now also remove the *cowering, nauseated, panicked,* and *stunned* conditions. This is the &quot&greater&quot& ability." imp14="You can instead expend one use of touch treatment to replicate the effects of ‹spell/break enchantment›. This targets only one creature you are touching and follows all other restrictions of touch treatment."}

driver.name: Driver
description: ## Driver
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/72›

A driver makes her living driving vehicles in the service of those who can pay for her considerable talents. She may be a wagon-driver practiced at smuggling contraband, an accomplished musher blazing arctic trails, or a seemingly refined coach driver placing her roguish talents in the service of a wealthy patron.

::ab[Hard Drive (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="When driving a vehicle that uses a group of animals or magical beasts as muscle propulsion, the DCs of all driving checks are reduced by 2, the maximum speed of the vehicle increases by 10 feet, and the acceleration increases by 5 feet." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Driver's Fortitude (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power flavor="The driver learns to keep driving and maintain control of her vehicle, even when mortally wounded." ability="If the driver drops below 0 hit points but is not dead, she can attempt a DC 15 Fortitude save each round to remain conscious and in control of her vehicle, though she may take no other actions." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Firearm training~getaway artist"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Feat~getaway master"}

earthshadow.name: Earthshadow
description: ## Earthshadow
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/8›

When the dwarves began their Quest for Sky millennia ago to reach the world's surface, they already had a deep understanding of the earth's secrets. In the centuries since, the mystic lore the dwarves call earthcraft has been passed down through the generations, and its practitioners are called earthshadows. While many earthshadows are dwarves, their secrets have been shared with a worthy few members of other races over the ages.

::ab[Earthlink (Su)]{flavor="The earth grants an earthshadow swiftness and hones her senses." icon=boost passive="When an earthshadow is in direct contact with an expanse of natural earth or unworked stone that is at least as large as she is, she adds half her rogue level (minimum 1) to Acrobatics and Perception checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Earthcraft (Su)]{l=2 icon=magic flavor="An earthshadow can draw upon the power of earth to perform mystical tricks." ability="At the start of each day, an earthshadow gains a pool of earthcraft points equal to half her rogue level plus her Wisdom modifier, and she can spend earthcraft points to use certain spell-like abilities. The caster level of these spell-like abilities is equal to the earthshadow's rogue level. Earthcraft effects affect only the earthshadow, even if the spell could normally affect others." imp2="She can spend 1 earthcraft point to cast ‹spell/blend›, even if she is not an elf, but this ability works only in areas of natural earth or unworked stone." imp4="An earthshadow can spend 2 earthcraft points to cast ‹spell/meld into stone›." imp8="An earthshadow can spend 3 earthcraft points to cast ‹spell/dimension door›. She must be able to see her destination (or she must be able to otherwise sense its surface, such as with ‹umr/tremorsense›), and her current location must be connected to her destination by continuous natural earth or unworked stone." imp12="An earthshadow can spend 5 earthcraft points to cast the spell ‹spell/stone tell› but can communicate only with unworked stone." imp16="An earthshadow can spend 6 earthcraft points to cast ‹spell/statue›." replace="Evasion~rogue talents gained at 4th, 8th, 12th, and 16th levels"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Extra earthcraft~favored terrain~fortified position~major magic~minor magic~resiliency~trap spotter"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Against the wall~dispelling attack~hide in plain sight~slippery mind~stony skin"}

eldritch_raider.name: Eldritch Raider
description: ## Eldritch Raider
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/189›

An eldritch raider is a rogue who seeks to unravel the mysteries of the destruction of the gillmen's homeland. They explore old ruins that date back to the days of the old human empire and track down relics and lore from its glory days. An eldritch raider has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Gillman}
::cskill{gain=ka~kh~spc lose=dis~perf~soh}
::ab[Skill Ranks per Level]{icon=down note="The eldritch raider gains only 6 + Int modifier skill points per level."}

::ab[Detect Magic (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic ability="An eldritch raider gains the ability to use ‹spell/detect magic› at will. The eldritch raider treats her rogue level as her caster level for this ability." special="This ability counts as the ‹talent/minor magic› rogue talent for purposes of qualifying for other rogue talents." replace="Rogue talent rogues gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Eldritch Intuition (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="An eldritch raider gains an intuitive sense that allows her to more easily activate sorcerer and wizard spell completion and spell trigger items. She gains a bonus on Use Magic Device checks for this purpose. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[New Talents]{head icon=power note="An eldritch raider has access to the following new advanced talents when selecting rogue advanced talents."}

::ab[Minor Eldritch Magic (Sp)]{next prereq="*Minor magic* and ‹talent/major magic› rogue talents; Intelligence 12" icon=magic ability="The eldritch raider gains the ability to cast a 2nd-level spell from the ‹main/spells>sorcerer»/wizard« spell list› two times per day as a spell-like ability. The caster level for this ability is equal to her rogue level. The save DC for this spell is 12 + her Intelligence modifier."}

::ab[Major Eldritch Magic (Sp)]{next icon=magic prereq="Minor Eldritch Magic; Intelligence 13" ability="The eldritch raider gains the ability to cast a 3rd-level spell from the sorcerer/wizard spell list two times per day as a spell-like ability. The caster level for this ability is equal to her rogue level. The save DC for this spell is 13 + her Intelligence modifier."}

@HL[These rogue talents complement this archetype:] ‹talent/Black market connections›, ‹talent/esoteric scholar›, ‹talent/fast picks›, major magic, minor magic, ‹talent/ninja trick› [‹ninjatrick/slow metabolism›, ‹ninjatrick/wall climber›], ‹talent/quick disable›, ‹talent/trap spotter›
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Dispelling attack~familiar~hard minded~improved evasion~slippery mind~thoughtful reexamining"}

eldritch_scoundrel.name: Eldritch Scoundrel
description: ## Eldritch Scoundrel
‹SOURCE Arcane Anthology/23›

Students of arcane magic, legerdemain, and stealth, eldritch scoundrels are a rare breed of adventurer most commonly found seeking lost and valuable arcane writings in the ruins of fallen empires, such as Thassilon or the Jistka Imperium.
::cskill{gain=ka~spc lose=diplo~dis}

::ab[Armor Proficiencies]{icon=down ability="An eldritch scoundrel is not proficient with any armor or shields. Armor sometimes interferes with an eldritch scoundrel's gestures, which can cause her spells with somatic components to fail." alter="Armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Skill Ranks]{icon=down note="An eldritch scoundrel gains 4 skill ranks (plus a number of ranks equal to her Intelligence modifier) at each level instead of a normal 8." alter=Skills}

::ab[Spells]{icon=magic-learn ability="An eldritch scoundrel casts arcane spells drawn from the ‹main/wizard spell« list›. An eldritch scoundrel must choose and prepare her spells ahead of time. She learns, prepares, and casts spells exactly as a ‹class/wizard› does, including adding new spells to her spellbook and gaining two additional spells known (of any level she can cast) each time she gains a rogue level with this archetype." special="An eldritch scoundrel can cast only a certain number of spells of each spell level per day. Her base daily spell allotment is the same as the ‹class/magus› class."}

::ab[Alarm Sense (Su)]{l=3 icon=power ability="An eldritch scoundrel gains an intuitive sense that warns her when she is near a magic trap. This functions as the ‹talent/trap spotter› rogue talent, but applies only if the eldritch scoundrel comes within 10 feet of a magic trap." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{icon=down l3="The eldritch scoundrel doesn't acquire the sneak attack class feature until 3rd level, when she gains a +1d6 sneak attack." increment="The sneak attack damage~3~4" incrementEnd="d6." alter="Sneak attack progression"}

::ab[Rogue Talents]{l=4 icon=power ability="The eldritch scoundrel gains a ‹ability/rogue talent› or ‹ability/ninja trick<s› for which she meets the prerequisites (treating her rogue level as her ninja level). If a talent requires her to expend points from her ki pool, she can instead expend a spell slot with a spell level equal to the number of ki points she would normally expend. If a talent functions only if she has ki in her ki pool, it functions as long as she still has a spell of 1st level or higher prepared." repeat="She can select another talent or trick.~8~4" imp12="From here on out, she can select an ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› in place of a rogue talent." alter="Rogue talent progression~advanced talent"}

::ab[Uncanny Training]{icon=info note="An eldritch scoundrel does not gain uncanny dodge or improved uncanny dodge." imp4="She can take ‹ability/uncanny dodge› instead of a rogue talent." imp12="The eldritch scoundrel can choose to take ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› in place of an advanced talent." alter="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

escapologist.name: Escapologist
description: ## Escapologist
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/123›
The escapologist excels at escaping any confinement, whether physical, magical, or mental. Few restraints can hold an escapologist for long.

::ab[Elusive]{icon=boost passive="An escapologist adds 1/2 her rogue level (minimum +1) as a bonus on all Disable Device and Escape Artist checks." order=pa ability="An escapologist can use Disable Device to disarm magical traps." special="This counts as *trapfinding* for the purpose of prerequisites and abilities that require trapfinding." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Unfettered Mind (Su)]{l=3 icon=def fullround="An escapologist can attempt an Escape Artist check to remove a mind-affecting effect currently affecting her. The DC of this check is equal to the effect's original save DC + 20. She cannot remove effects that do not allow a saving throw. She can attempt to escape even if paralyzed or restrained, as long as she can take purely mental actions." special="She can attempt this check against a particular effect once per round if the effect is measured in rounds, once per minute if the effect is measured in minutes, once per 10 minutes if the effect is measured in 10-minute intervals, and so on." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Impossible Escape (Su)]{l=8 icon=def immediate="An escapologist can attempt an Escape Artist check at a -10 penalty and apply the result in place of either a failed Reflex save or her AC against an attack that would have hit her. If she succeeds, her contortions let her avoid the attack or effect entirely." special="Using this ability leaves the escapologist ‹misc/staggered› until the end of her next turn. If the escapologist can't become staggered, she can't use impossible escape." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

false_medium.name: False Medium
description: ## False Medium
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/123›

The false medium is a master of pretending to have occult powers in order to manipulate the emotions and atmosphere of the people and places around him, often reaping a healthy reward from the bereaved, who find solace in his comforting lies.

::ab[Dim the Lights (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="While in ‹rule/dim light› or darkness, a false medium adds 1/2 his rogue level as a bonus on Bluff, Disguise, and Sleight of Hand checks, and creatures without low-light vision or darkvision take an equal penalty on skill checks to oppose the false medium's Bluff, Disguise, and Sleight of Hand checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[False Sensitivity (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A charlatan can convincingly fake the ability to use occult skill unlocks such as ‹skill/automatic writing› and ‹skill/dowsing›. An untrained onlooker can't tell the difference between the false medium's scams and a true use of occult skill unlocks. An onlooker capable of using the particular occult skill unlock that the false medium is faking can attempt the appropriate skill check for that unlock, opposed by the false medium's Bluff check, to detect the fraud. In any case, any results of the false medium's charades are fabricated (for instance, the false medium might ask leading questions to elicit false memories while pretending to use hypnotism)." replace="2nd-level rogue talent"}

::ab[Haunting Presences (Ex)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="The false medium exploits his natural talent for deception to convince others of the presence of false supernatural entities." ability="As long as the false medium is in conditions of dim light or darkness, he can use Bluff skill checks to emulate the effects of ‹spell/dancing lights›, ‹spell/ghost sound›, ‹spell/silent image›, or ‹spell/ventriloquism› by throwing his voice, subtly misdirecting or convincing subjects, or manipulating light and shadows. The range of these effects is 10 feet from the false medium. The effects last for 1 round, though they can be maintained with additional checks." special="Only one of these effects can be created or maintained in a given round. Observers can attempt either a Perception or Sense Motive check (whichever of their bonuses is higher) to oppose this Bluff check; the GM should roll these checks secretly. This is an extraordinary ability and not an illusion, so if the false medium succeeds at the opposed check, even powerful effects like ‹spell/true seeing› don't detect the fakery." replace="Trap sense" incrementPlain="The range of his effects~3~3~15~5" incrementEnd=" feet from him." incrementDesc="can now reach up to~increase to~now extend to reach"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Esoteric scholar~fast fingers~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic~quick trapsmith"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Feat~master of disguise~skill mastery"}

fey_prankster.name: Fey Prankster
description: ## Fey Prankster
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/12›
Fey pranksters specialize in tricking others to teach a lesson or for their own entertainment.
::prereq{r=Gathlain}

::ab[Treacherous Plants (Sp)]{icon=power ability="The fey prankster can cause plants to distract nearby creatures. To use this ability, the fey prankster attempts a Bluff check to create a distraction to hide as a @HLswift_action or to ‹rule/feint› against an adjacent opponent as a @HLmove_action, so long as the target is adjacent to plants. He gains a bonus on this check equal to half his rogue level (minimum +1)." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Improved Dirty Trick]{l=2 icon=power ability="The fey prankster gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat. He need not meet the prerequisites." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Steal Appearance (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="The fey prankster can exchange the appearance of two adjacent items of the same size. He can also exchange the appearance of two creatures if at least one is willing and both are adjacent. Affecting an unwilling creature or an item it wears or carries requires a successful ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver against that creature. If he succeeds, the objects or creatures are each disguised as the other as if with ‹spell/veil› cast at his rogue level. The Will save DC to resist or disbelieve the illusion is equal to 10 + half the rogue's level + his Intelligence modifier." imp8="The fey prankster can also exchange the locations of the two targets by succeeding at a DC 20 Sleight of Hand check. If he succeeds, observers can't tell which is the original based on movement without succeeding at a Perception check against his Sleight of Hand result." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Greater Dirty Trick]{l=6 icon=power ability="The fey prankster gains ‹feat/Greater Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat. He does not need to meet the prerequisites." replace="Rogue talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Plant Traps (Su)]{l=8 icon=power usage="3 times/day" fullround="The fey trickster can modify a plant (but not plant creatures) into a trap. The Perception and Disable Device DCs for the trap are each equal to 15 + his rogue level. A Tiny or larger plant can be used to perform a ‹rule/trip› or dirty trick combat maneuver using the fey trickster's CMB and the plant's size. A Large or larger plant can be used to create a ‹spell/snare› as per the spell. The save DC is equal to 12 + the rogue's Intelligence modifier." special="The fey trickster can trigger any plant trap he created as a @HLstandard_action as long as he is within 60 feet of it." replace="Rogue talent gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Unseen Trickster]{l=12 icon=power ability="The fey trickster can attempt Stealth checks in areas with plants even if he does not have ‹rule/cover› or ‹rule/concealment›." replace="Rogue talent gained at 12th level"}

filcher.name: Filcher
description: ## Filcher
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/64›

A filcher steals valuables without their owners even realizing it. Whether cutting purses in the midst of combat or replacing prized items with fakes under the noses of their owners, the filcher is the master of the quick and quiet steal. A filcher has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Halfling}

::ab[Quicker than the Eye (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower-boost flavor="A filcher develops an amazingly swift and delicate touch." ability="When she uses Sleight of Hand, creatures take a penalty on their Perception checks to notice the attempt equal to half the filcher's class level. The filcher also subtracts her class level from the normal -20 penalty when attempting to make a Sleight of Hand check as a @HLmove_action instead of as a standard action. Lastly, the filcher can withdraw an object hidden on her person, including a weapon, as a move action instead of the usual standard action." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Rummage (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A filcher learns how to assess the value of items at the quickest glance. She can even make startlingly accurate guesses about particular items merely by observing the bulges they make in pouches, backpacks, or similar containers." passive="She gains a bonus on Appraise checks equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level." swift="A filcher can make an Appraise check in order to determine the relative value of each object carried by her target (DC = 10 + 1 for every object the filcher is trying to ascertain the relative value of). Though she never learns the actual prices of items when using *rummage,* she does gain enough information to list these items in order, from the most valuable to the least valuable. She can, by taking a -20 penalty on the check, add to this assessment any items carried by her target that she cannot see." replace="Trap sense +1, +2, +3, +4, +5, and +6" order=pw}

::ab[Filch (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-boost flavor="A filcher learns how pluck items off her opponents even in combat." ability="She gains ‹feat/Improved Steal› as a bonus feat and can use her Sleight of Hand bonus instead of her CMB when performing a ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver. If the filcher gains bonuses on combat maneuver checks from any feats, spells, magic items, or similar effects, they are added to the Sleight of Hand bonus when using the steal maneuver." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Superior Filching (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power-lower flavor="A filcher becomes a master at separating owners from their property." ability="She gains ‹feat/Greater Steal› as a bonus feat, and opponents do not gain a +5 bonus to their CMD when she tries to remove items fastened to them." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~deft palm~fast fingers~fast getaway~fast stealth~slow reactions"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Fast tumble~skill mastery~weapon snatcher"}

galtan_agitator.name: Galtan Agitator
description: ## Galtan Agitator
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/39›

Galtan agitators fight mightily to cast down the enemies of their ideals within the shambles of the tumultuous nation of Galt. Any rogue (including unchained rogues) can select the Galtan agitator archetype.

::ab[Reputation (Ex)]{icon=power ability="This ability functions as the ‹soctalent/renown› vigilante social talent. In addition to improving attitudes, it also improves the Galtan agitator's starting ‹rule/influence› and reputation with ‹rule/contacts› in the area by one level or step." special="For the purpose of vigilante social talents, the Galtan agitator does not have a vigilante identity and is always considered to be in her social identity, unless she has another identity (such as from the ‹class/vigilante› class). If the Galtan agitator has another identity, she does not gain any benefits from *renown* while in that identity unless she has the *renown* social talent or a similar ability." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Ready for Betrayal (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A Galtan agitator gains a bonus on Perception checks to recognize disguises and notice hiding creatures and on Sense Motive checks to disbelieve lies. @B-3fp-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Enthralling Agitation (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A Galtan agitator can captivate a crowd once per day, as per the spell ‹spell/enthrall› (DC = 10 + 1/2 the Galtan agitator's rogue level + her Charisma modifier). During the spell's effect, the Galtan agitator can attempt a DC 15 Charisma check to increase her individual or organizational influence with the members of the crowd by 1 rank or step. Or, if the crowd members' attitudes toward the Galtan agitator are indifferent or worse, this check can improve their attitudes by 1 step." special="The Galtan agitator can never use this ability to increase her individual or organization influence or improve the crowd's attitude by more than 2 ranks or steps." replace="Rogue talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Revolutionary's Cause (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic flavor="The Galtan agitator can lift an enthralled crowd's emotions and turn them toward a common purpose." standard="After she has used her *enthralling agitation,* but before the *enthrall* effect wears off, she can target the affected creatures with a plausible ‹spell/suggestion› (DC = 10 + 1/2 her rogue level + her Charisma modifier) that has a duration of up to 1 day." replace="Rogue talent gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Leadership (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power-boost ability="A Galtan agitator gains ‹feat/Leadership› as a bonus feat. If the Galtan agitator already has the Leadership feat, she doubles her Charisma modifier when calculating her base Leadership score." replace="Rogue talent gained at 12th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Canny observer~coax information~follow clues~hard to fool~strong impression"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Feat~skill mastery~thoughtful reexamining"}

guerrilla.name: Guerrilla
description: ## Guerrilla
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/41›

Guerrillas fight against oppressive leadership and governments - often under cover of night - including Chelish colonization in Sargava and the power structures in Cheliax proper, Nidal, and Razmiran. Any rogue (including the unchained rogue) can select the guerrilla archetype.

::ab[Skilled Liar (Ex)]{icon=boost-power ability="Whenever a guerrilla uses Bluff to attempt to deceive someone, she gains a bonus on the opposed roll equal to 1/2 her rogue level (minimum +1). A guerrilla can still use Disable Device to disarm magic traps." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Cover of Night (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-def flavor="A guerrilla learns to use darkness to her advantage." passive="She gains a +5 bonus on Disguise, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth checks while she is in areas of ‹rule/dim light› or darkness. Additionally, while in dim light or darkness, if a guerrilla would have ‹rule/concealment›, she instead has total concealment." order=pr fullround="She can create mundane disguises in dim light or darkness (at a faster rate than the normal 1d3 &times& 10 minutes)." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Secret Messenger (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A guerrilla gains a bonus on Bluff checks to convey secret messages and on Sense Motive checks to discern secret messages. @B-3fp-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Guerrilla Sniping (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When a guerrilla is in an area of dim light or darkness, she takes only a -10 penalty on her Stealth check to maintain her obscured location while sniping." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Uncanny Dodge (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A guerrilla gains ‹ability/uncanny dodge›." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~fast stealth~powerful sneak~rogue crawl~stand up~surprise attack"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deadly sneak~master of disguise~opportunist~stealthy sniper"}

guild_agent.name: Guild Agent
description: ## Guild Agent
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/45›

Guild agents are members of a thieves' guild or other shadowy organization. What they lose in independence, they more than make up for in benefits gained due to their association's infrastructure. Guild agents come at all levels of the organization, from a lowly recruit all the way up to the guildmaster himself.

::ab[Honor Among Thieves (Ex)]{icon=warning note="A guild agent must remain a member in good standing of a thieves' guild (he starts at rank 1 with her guild when using the ‹rule/organizational influence› system). If the guild agent leaves his guild, he loses all of the abilities granted by this archetype and does not gain their replacements. If he joins a new guild, the guild agent regains his abilities, though depending on how he joins the guild, he may regain his abilities all at once or gradually over time. Even if the guild in question operates in multiple locations, the guild agent chooses a settlement as a base of operations for his own activities."}

::ab[Guild Connections (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost ability="A guild agent gains the ‹talent/black market connections› rogue talent. Furthermore, he can ask his guild for tips about the local scene, granting him a bonus equal to half his rogue level on Knowledge (local) checks and Diplomacy checks to gather information, as long as the information in question pertains to the guild agent's base of operations." imp4="The guild agent also gains the ‹soctalent/gossip collector› vigilante social talent and his base of operations counts as his area of renown for the purpose of determining the effects of gossip collector." replace="Evasion~uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Pull Rank (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A guild agent has attained sufficient rank to call in lesser guild members to assist him with a job. He can call lesser members of a number and level equal to the number of followers he would have if he had the ‹feat/Leadership› feat (including the +2 bonus for his guildhouse), to a maximum of a number of lesser members equal to his rogue level. If the guild agent possesses the Leadership feat, he gains twice as many followers as normal. If he spends a favor, he can combine this with the command team organizational influence benefit to gain even more lesser members for a particularly difficult job. Each time the guild agent uses the lesser members recklessly and gets them killed, he loses at least 1 influence with his guild." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Criminal Mastermind (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="Except in an unusually powerful guild, a guild agent has become the true power behind the guild. The guild agent has built up a nearly worldwide reputation under a particular criminal epithet (such as &quot&The Silver Shadow&quot&), known by criminals and law enforcement officers everywhere. This works like the ‹soctalent/renown› vigilante social talent except as follows. It applies in all towns and cities on the same continent as the guild agent's base of operations, without requiring him to select a particular settlement. He has only one identity, and only other criminals and members of the underworld treat their attitudes toward the guild agent as one step closer to helpful at all times (rather than while only in his social identity), while the bonus on Intimidate checks applies against everyone at all times (rather than while only in his vigilante identity)." replace="Master strike"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Assault leader~certainty|unchained rogue only~getaway artist"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Getaway master~hunter's surprise~opportunist~rumormonger~skill mastery"}

gun_smuggler.name: Gun Smuggler
description: ## Gun Smuggler
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/13›

Alkenstar protects the secrets of gunpowder and controls the sale of firearms, creating lucrative opportunities for those who can sneak the technology beyond the Mana Wastes. Gun smugglers are adept at procuring firearms and concealing their secret weapons.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=power ability="A gun smuggler is proficient with all simple weapons and one-handed firearms, plus the ‹eq-weapon/rapier›, ‹eq-weapon/sap›, ‹eq-weapon/short sword›, and ‹eq-weapon/sword cane›." replace="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Hidden Gun (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A gun smuggler gains a bonus equal to half her rogue level on Sleight of Hand checks to conceal a one-handed firearm on her body. She increases the DCs of opponents' Perception checks to realize the true nature of disguised weapons (such as a sword cane) within 5 feet of her by an amount equal to her rogue level (maximum DC 25 plus her rogue level)." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Secret Sidearm (Ex)]{icon=power ability="As an expert in discreet and easily concealable weapons and firearms, a gun smuggler gains a battered gun identical to that gained by a 1st-level ‹class/gunslinger› and ‹feat/Gunsmithing› as a bonus feat (including the ability to restore her battered gun as if she were a gunslinger), though she must select a ‹eq-weapon/coat pistol›, ‹eq-weapon/dagger pistol›, or ‹eq-weapon/sword cane pistol› as her battered weapon."}

::ab[Selective Targeting (Ex)]{icon=down ability="A gun smuggler focuses on concealable firearms for her sneak attacks. When she makes a sneak attack using any weapon other than a coat pistol, dagger pistol, or sword cane pistol (either firing the weapon or making a melee attack), her sneak attack damage dice are d4s instead of d6s." alter="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Stolen Shots (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A gun smuggler can access small quantities of cutting-edge firearm technology through clandestine dealers. She gains the benefits of the ‹talent/black market connections› rogue talent, though only for the purpose of securing firearms and related equipment. At the beginning of each day, the gun smuggler gains a number of bullets (with accompanying black powder) or paper cartridges - referred to as shots - in any combination equal to her rogue level, representing ammunition smuggled to her by contacts. Her contacts provide only enough shots to replenish this small supply, so she can't accumulate ammunition indefinitely, and these shots have a market price of 0 gp." imp6="The gun smuggler can elect to gain one or more pieces of magic ammunition, though each one replaces a number of her daily shots equal to the ammunition's enhancement bonus (maximum enhancement bonus equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her level for any one piece of ammunition). She can choose to add any of the following weapon special abilities to the shot, though the shot must have at least a +1 enhancement bonus, and these special abilities use an amount of bonus equal to the ability's normal price: ‹magic-enh/distance›, ‹magic-enh/flaming›, ‹magic-enh/flaming burst›, ‹magic-enh/merciful›, ‹magic-enh/seeking›, or ‹magic-enh/thundering›." replace="Trap sense~rogue talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Uncanny Aim (Ex)]{icon=boost l4="A gun smuggler increases the range increment of any coat pistol, dagger pistol, or sword cane pistol she wields by 10 feet." l8="The base damage of bullets fired by these weapons increases by ‹misc/one die step›." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}

heister.name: Heister
description: ## Heister
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/45›

Notorious for their stealth, imagination, and elaborately planned thefts and capers, heisters specialize in breaking into secure locations, stealing items of value, and escaping undetected. Heisters often work as members of or independent operatives contracted to criminal organizations, crime families, or wealthy individuals inclined to unlawful activity.

::ab[Rum Dubber (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="Whenever a heister attempts to open a lock without a set of ‹eq-misc/thieves' tools›, the DC increases by 2, instead of 10." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Ferret's Grace (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A heister gains ‹feat/Stealthy› as a bonus feat. Additionally, a heister counts as a creature one size category smaller than she actually is when determining whether or not she is ‹misc/squeezing› into an enclosed space. Finally, when a heister uses the ‹skill/Escape Artist› skill to move through a tight space where her head fits but her shoulders do not, the DC is 20, instead of 30." replace="Uncanny dodge~rogue talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Unseen (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A heister masters hiding in underground and urban terrain. She can use the Stealth skill to hide in underground and urban terrain, even while being observed. She also gains the ‹talent/terrain mastery› rogue talent twice, once each for underground and urban terrain." replace="Improved uncanny dodge~rogue talent gained at 8th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Certainty|unchained rogue only~expert leaper~fast getaway~fast picks~fast stealth~getaway artist~ledge walker~quick disable"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Cutting edge|unchained rogue only~fast tumble~getaway master~hidden mind~skill mastery"}

investigator.name: Investigator
description: ## Investigator
>***Note:** This is a rogue archetype, and has no relation to the ‹class/investigator› hybrid class.*
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/133›

Not all rogues work against the law. Investigators use their skills to serve the law, often in the employ of nobles or in the pursuit of noble causes. In some cities cabals of investigators work for rulers or bureaucracies, but often an investigator is a free agent who pursues whatever mysteries come across her path. Of course, not all investigators serve the law. Crime lords and guildmasters often have squads of investigators serving their own nefarious purposes.

::ab[Follow Up (Ex)]{icon=roll-boost ability="An investigator can roll twice on any Diplomacy check made to gather information, and receives the information for both results. This takes the same amount of time as one check. If the lesser of the two checks reveals false information, the rogue is aware of it. False information is not revealed in this way if the people she questioned do not know it to be false." replace=Trapfinding}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Coax information~fast picks~follow clues~hard to fool~minor magic"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hunter's surprise~slippery mind~thoughtful reexamining"}

kintargo_rebel.name: Kintargo Rebel
description: ## Kintargo Rebel
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/40›

With few natural barriers, a thriving international port, and many miles separating it from Egorian, Kintargo is geographically suited for rebellion against the rigid laws that reign in Cheliax. This rebellion is hardly malicious in intent; business is simply easier without the imposing bureaucracy. Whenever the Chelish navy sails into the port, these rebels are quick to appear agreeably subservient in order to conceal their non-compliance and avoid retribution.

::ab[Sophisticated Stealth (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A Kintargo rebel gains a bonus on Knowledge (nobility) checks. In addition, she gains a bonus on Bluff checks to convey a secret message and on Sense Motive checks to discern secret messages. @B-3fp-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Misdirection (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A Kintargo rebel gains the ability to cast ‹spell/misdirection› on herself once per day, though instead of choosing an object for the second target, she must choose a creature in range. The caster level for this ability is equal to her class level, and the save DC for this spell is 12 + the Kintargo rebel's Intelligence modifier. The save DC of this ability increases by 1 if the secondary target is lawful or evil." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Canny observer~charmer~false friend~fast getaway~honeyed words~obfuscate story~quick disguise"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hard minded~master of disguise~skill mastery~slippery mind~thoughtful reexamining"}

kitsune_trickster.name: Kitsune Trickster
description: ## Kitsune Trickster
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/192›

The kitsune trickster combines her sharpened wit with minor arcane powers of charm and persuasion. She uses her talents to spin convincing lies, riddles, and stories. A trickster has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Kitsune}

::ab[Kitsune's Guile (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="A trickster relies on her intellect as much as her personality." passive="She adds her Intelligence modifier on Bluff, Diplomacy, Disguise, and Sense Motive checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Kitsune's Charm (Sp)]{icon=magic l3="A kitsune trickster can use ‹spell/charm person› as a spell-like ability (caster level equal to her rogue level - 2)." useInc=rogue~3 useF="3~1" replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Charmer~coax information~convincing lie~false friend~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic~obfuscate story~steal the story"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Master of disguise~rumormonger~skill mastery~slippery mind"}

knife_master.name: Knife Master
description: ## Knife Master
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/72›

The knife master is a trained killer who specializes in close-up combat and the wave and weave of knife fighting. In her hands, daggers and other similar light blades become truly deadly instruments..
::ab[Hidden Blade]{icon=boost passive="A knife master adds 1/2 her level on Sleight of Hand checks made to conceal a light blade." replace=Trapfinding}
