::ab[Sneak Stab (Ex)]{icon=boost-down note="A knife master focuses her ability to deal sneak attack damage with daggers and similar weapons to such a degree that she can deal more sneak attack damage with those weapons at the expense of sneak attacks with other weapons." order=na ability="When she makes a sneak attack with a ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, ‹eq-weapon/kerambit›, ‹eq-weapon/kukri›, ‹eq-weapon/punching dagger«s›, ‹eq-weapon/starknife›, or ‹eq-weapon/swordbreaker dagger›, she uses d8s to roll sneak attack damage instead of d6s. For sneak attacks with all other weapons, she uses d4s instead of d6s." special="This ability is identical in all other ways to sneak attack, and supplements that ability." alter="Sneak attack"}

::ab[Blade Sense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A knife master is so skilled in combat involving light blades that she gains a dodge bonus to AC against attacks made against her with light blades. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Befuddling strike~combat trick~offensive defense~surprise attack~underhanded~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~confounding blades~deadly sneak~entanglement of blades~unwitting ally"}

makeshift_scrapper.name: Makeshift Scrapper
description: ## Makeshift Scrapper
‹SOURCE Melee Tactics Toolbox/15›
Some combatants specialize in the study of certain weapons, but those without formal training often learn to survive a fight by making brilliant use of whatever is lying around.
::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=down note="The makeshift scrapper is proficient with only simple weapons."}
::ab[Improvised Weapons (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A makeshift scrapper gains ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› and ‹feat/Throw Anything› as bonus feats." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Supernatural Improvisation (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost-def useL=rogue useNC flavor="A makeshift scrapper gains the ability to enhance an item, making it a more effective weapon or changing the way it can be used." swift="She can alter a single ‹misc/improvised weapon› to either grant it a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls or use it to gain a +1 shield bonus to her AC." special="The makeshift scrapper can change the enhancement each time she activates the ability." increment="The bonus~3~4" replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[Improvised Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="The makeshift scrapper gains ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery› as a bonus feat." replace="Rogue talent gained at 12th level"}

@HL[These rogue talents complement this archetype:] ‹talent/Expert leaper›, ‹talent/fast getaway›, ‹talent/ki pool›, ‹talent/offensive defense›, ‹talent/ninja trick› [‹ninjatrick/acrobatic master›], ‹talent/peerless maneuver›, ‹talent/surprise attack›
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~defensive roll~getaway master~opportunist"}

master_of_disguise.name: Master of Disguise
description: ## Master of Disguise
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/46›

A master of disguise feels no need to advertise her skills to build a reputation. Instead, she believes that never letting her adversaries know her true identity protects her from bad luck or missteps on a job.

::ab[Consummate Actor (Ex)]{icon=boost-power l1="A master of disguise adds half her rogue level (minimum 1) on all Disguise checks and on Bluff checks to stay in character while using Disguise." l2="She gains the ‹talent/quick disguise› rogue talent, and she can create a disguise twice as quickly as normal even for that rogue talent (she can create a disguise that encompasses only minor details as a @HLstandard_action)." replace="Trapfinding~rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Grandmaster of Disguise (Ex)]{l=10 icon=power ability="A master of disguise gains the ‹talent/master of disguise› advanced rogue talent and can use it an unlimited number of times per day. Because she must still don a physical disguise to gain this bonus, it doesn't stack with the bonuses from spells like ‹spell/alter self› and ‹spell/disguise self›, but since it is completely nonmagical, it is more reliable when scrutinized under magical detection." replace="Rogue talent gained at 10th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~false friend~major magic~minor magic~obfuscate story~shades of gray~steal the story"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hard minded~hidden mind~skill mastery~slippery mind~unwitting ally"}

nameless_shadow.name: Nameless Shadow
description: ## Nameless Shadow
‹SOURCE Merchant's Manifest/30›

Where bandits and tyrants prey on merchants and caravans, the nameless shadow preys on bandits and tyrants. Posing as mundane merchants or other harmless travelers, these assassins can hide both within darkness and in the anonymity of a seemingly unimportant crowd. This archetype is appropriate for rogues built using the rules from the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook as well as for unchained rogues.

::ab[Harmless Guise (Ex)]{head icon=power ability="A nameless shadow gains the dual identity and *seamless guise* ‹class/vigilante› class features, with the following restrictions. Her social identity must be that of a mundane and non-threatening member of society, such as a farmer or a merchant." imp6="The nameless shadow gains the ‹soctalent/many guises› vigilante social talent." imp8="The nameless shadow gains the ‹soctalent/quick change› vigilante social talent." imp14="The nameless shadow gains the ‹soctalent/everyman› vigilante social talent." imp18="The nameless shadow gains the ‹soctalent/any guise› vigilante social talent." special="Additionally, when a nameless shadow attacks a foe while in a social or mundane identity, her target takes a -2 penalty to AC for the purpose of resolving that attack. This use of *harmless guise* forces the nameless shadow into her vigilante identity after the attack is completed, and the target (if it survives) then becomes aware of the nameless shadow's secret. This ability cannot be used on targets that are aware of the nameless shadow's dual identity." replace="Trapfinding~rogue talents gained at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels"}
:::ab{title="Dual Identity (Ex)" flavor="Text from the vigilante class" next icon=power-def action=Ability}

You hide your true identity, allowing you to move about social circles and nobility without carrying the stigma of your ruthless actions. In effect, you have two identities: one is a polite member of society while the other is a skilled and cunning warrior. To keep up this charade, you usually have two names: your true name, used in polite company, and your vigilante name, used to strike fear in the hearts of those who oppose you. Knowledge checks about one do not reveal information about the other, unless your true identity is revealed to the world at large.

You can start each day in either of your identities, referred to simply as @HL[social] or @HL[vigilante]. Changing from one identity to another takes 1 minute and must be done out of sight from other creatures to preserve your secret. Changing identities is more than just changing outfits and clothing (although that is certainly a part of it); the process often also involves applying make-up, altering your hair, and adjusting other personal effects. Furthermore, the change is as much a state of mind as of body, so items such as a ‹magic-wondrous/hat of disguise› and similar spells and effects that change the user's appearance do not reduce the time required to change identities. Most *social talents* require you to be in your social identity, but a vigilante who uses *vigilante talents* in his social identity risks exposing his secret.

Despite being a single person, your dual nature allows you to have two alignments, one for each of your identities. When in an identity, you are treated as having that identity's alignment for all spells, magic items, and abilities that rely on alignment. For the purpose of meeting a qualification for a feat, class, or any ability, you are only eligible if both of your alignments meet the requirements. A vigilante's two alignments cannot be more than one step from each other on a single alignment axis. For example, a vigilante with a lawful neutral social identity could have a vigilante identity that is lawful good, lawful neutral, lawful evil, neutral, neutral good, or neutral evil. If you are the target of an effect that would change your alignment, it changes both of your alignments to the new alignment.

Any attempts to scry or otherwise locate you work only if you are currently in the identity the creature is attempting to locate (or if the creature knows that the two identities are the same individual). Otherwise, the spell or effect has no effect, revealing nothing but darkness, as if the target were invalid or did not exist.

::ab[Seamless Guise (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the vigilante class" icon=def ability="A vigilante knows how to behave in a way that appears perfectly proper and normal for his current identity. Should anyone suspect you of being anything other than what you appear to be while either in your social or vigilante identity, you can attempt a Disguise check with a +20 circumstance bonus to appear as your current identity, and not as your other identity."}

::ab[Face in the Crowd (Ex)]{icon=boost-power l4="So long as there are at least 10 other non-hostile creatures within 30 feet of her, a nameless shadow can use Bluff or Disguise in place of Stealth to hide or for sniping." l8="She can use this ability to hide within a crowd even if she is being observed." replace="Rogue talents gained at 4th and 8th levels"}

needler.name: Needler
description: ## Needler
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/12›

While some assassins and killers prefer to be up front with their poison use, others favor a covert approach. Needlers use subtlety and misdirection to deliver deadly poisons to unsuspecting victims in creative and unorthodox ways. This archetype is appropriate for rogues built using the rules from the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook as well as for unchained rogues.

::ab[Adroit Poisoner (Ex)]{icon=boost l2="A needler gains a +2 bonus on Sleight of Hand checks. This bonus increases to +4 when the needler uses Sleight of Hand to draw a hidden weapon that is coated in poison." l8="These bonuses increase to +4 and +6, respectively." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Subtle Poisoning (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-boost ability="A needler can apply a contact or ingested poison to her weapon and deliver it as if it were an injury poison. The poison must be delivered within 1 minute of this application, or it is wasted. The DC of the saving throw to resist the poison decreases by 1 when the needler uses this ability to deliver a contact or ingested poison via injury." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Needle Poisoner (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power swift="A needler can apply a dose of poison to a weapon." special="This ability counts as *swift poisoning* for the purpose of meeting prerequisites.@FN1" replace="Rogue talent gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Concealed Delivery (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power flavor="A needler learns how to deliver all types of poisons with subtlety." standard="She can deliver an ingested or inhaled poison to a creature within reach by placing a dose of poison on the target's lips, on the target's neck, or through other similar contact with the target's skin as if the poison were a contact poison. Delivering poison in this way requires a successful Sleight of Hand check opposed by the target's Perception check. The poison is not lost on a failed check, but it must be successfully delivered within 1 minute of the first attempt, or it is wasted." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[The only ability named "swift poisoning" is the ‹class/alchemist› class feature.]{from=1}

numerian_scavenger.name: Numerian Scavenger
description: ## Numerian Scavenger
‹SOURCE Technology Guide/13›

A Numerian scavenger lives for the discovery of new technological wonders scraped from the depths of the strange ruins, metal caverns, and outlandish junkyards of Numeria. She is well-versed in all the ways that technology can be used to protect sites, as well as the ways in which it can malfunction.

::ab[Technic Training (Ex)]{icon=boost-power-down order=pa passive="A Numerian scavenger adds 1/2 her rogue level to Perception skill checks to locate mechanical or high-tech traps and to Disable Device skill checks against these traps (minimum +1)." ability="She gains ‹feat/Technologist› as a bonus feat." special="She cannot use Disable Device to disarm magical traps." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Lucky Glitch (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def ability="Whenever a Numerian scavenger triggers a ‹rule/glitch› when using a timeworn technological item, she rolls twice and can choose which of the two glitch effects actually occurs. A Numerian scavenger always adds her level to rolls to determine what kind of glitch occurs, and treats rolls of over 100 as 100." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Robot Slayer (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost flavor="A Numerian scavenger knows just where to strike robots to incapacitate and disable them." ability="When she deals sneak attack damage to a robot, she ignores all hardness that robot possesses." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

okeno_liberator.name: Okeno Liberator
description: ## Okeno Liberator
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/41›

The sight of yellow sails on the Inner Sea signals the approach of a ship from Okeno, home port of the most ruthless and feared of all slaving operations. Most who see these sails flee in terror, but some freedom fighters actually endeavor to be captured, knowing that being taken to Stonespine Island in manacles is the easiest way to infiltrate the city, liberate other slaves, and lead them to freedom.

::ab[Bond Breaker (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="An Okeno liberator adds 1/2 her class level to Escape Artist checks, and never takes a penalty on Disable Device checks when using improvised tools to open locks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Covert Commander (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="An Okeno liberator grants all allies within 30 feet who are in her line of sight (but not herself) a competence bonus on Disguise and Stealth checks. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[Catch Off-Guard (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power flavor="The Okeno liberator is adept at improvising weapons." ability="She gains ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard› as a bonus feat." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deft palm~fast picks~fast stealth~quick disguise~slow reactions~underhanded"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Defensive roll~fast tumble~knock-out blow~skill mastery~weapon snatcher"}

phantom_thief.name: Phantom Thief
description: ## Phantom Thief
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/46›

Also known as gentleman thieves or lady thieves, phantom thieves don't know the desperation of a hard life like many rogues. Instead, they come from the ranks of the elite, having grown bored with their finery, and seek thrills from acts of daring, skill, and crime. Phantom thieves don't need the money, so they often perform acts such as breaking into a secure vault for the thrill, leaving a calling card rather than robbing the place.
::cskill{gain=ha~heal~ride~spc~sur~ka~kd~ke~kg~kh~kl~kna~kno~kp~kr}

::ab[Refined Education (Ex)]{icon=boost l1="The phantom thief selects one of her rogue class skills and adds half her rogue level on all skill checks using that skill." repeat="She selects an additional rogue class skill and adds half her rogue level on all skill checks using that skill as well.~3~2" imp4="She gains *rogue's edge* skill unlocks with each of these skills rather than selecting one skill every 5 levels, and she adds half her rogue level to her number of ranks to determine when she receives a skill unlock (so a 7th level rogue (phantom thief) with 7 ranks in a chosen skill would count as having 10 ranks and receive the second skill unlock)." replace="Debilitating injury~sneak attack~trapfinding" alter="rogue's edge (unchained rogue only)"}

::ab[Broad Education (Ex)]{flavor="A phantom thief's broad studies with expensive tutors keep her up to speed in both martial and magical knowledge, as well as in her skills." icon=power ability="She can select the ‹talent/combat trick›, ‹talent/minor magic›, and ‹talent/major magic› rogue talents as many times as she likes, and she can select the ‹feat/Skill Focus› feat as a rogue talent, also as many times as she likes. Finally, she can select a vigilante ‹ability/social talent<s› instead of a rogue talent, except for *social grace* and vigilante social talents that would require her to be a craftsman or professional." special="For the purpose of vigilante social talents, a phantom thief does not have a vigilante identity and is always considered to be in her social identity." alter="Rogue talent"}

::ab[Social Sense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A phantom thief's social sixth sense allows her to avoid danger in social situations and to act first when it breaks out." passive="She receives a bonus on Sense Motive checks to avoid being surprised by a foe, on Bluff checks to surprise a foe, and on initiative checks for surprise rounds that involved Bluff and Sense Motive checks to determine surprise. @B-3fp-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Master of All (Ex)]{l=20 icon=roll ability="A phantom thief can reroll any skill check as long as it is a class skill in which she is trained. She must take the second result, even if it is worse." usage="Once per minute" replace="Master strike"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Certainty|unchained rogue only~combat trick~major magic~minor magic"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Cutting edge|unchained rogue only~feat~hidden mind~rumormonger~skill mastery"}

pirate.name: Pirate
description: ## Pirate
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/73›

A pirate breaks from the confines of country and king to commit her crimes upon the high seas. She holds allegiance only to her ship and its captain (if even that much is true), and lives a lawless life upon the waves, plundering ships and shorelines as suits her whim.

::ab[Sea Legs (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power flavor="A pirate becomes adept at moving on ships, boats, and similar vessels." ability="She gains the ‹feat/Sea Legs› feat as a bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Swinging Reposition (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A pirate incorporates a ship's masts, rigging, ropes, sails, and other such structures into her combat style. Provided she is wearing light armor or no armor, when fighting in an environment where such structures exist, the rogue incorporates them into her movement, and does not have to move in a straight line when making either a ‹rule/charge› attack or a ‹rule/bull rush› combat maneuver." special="Once she completes her attack or maneuver, she can reposition herself. Immediately after making the charge or bull rush, she can move 5 feet as a @HLfree_action, even if the charge ends her turn. This movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="2nd-level rogue talent"}

::ab[Unflinching (Ex)]{flavor="Pirates are a salty and steadfast lot." icon=def l3="A pirate gains a bonus on saving throws against fear and mind-affecting effects. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~combat trick~finesse rogue~firearm training~hold breath~rope master~strong stroke"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Getaway master~hard minded~unwitting ally"}

planar_sneak.name: Planar Sneak
description: ## Planar Sneak
‹SOURCE Planes of Power/8›
A planar sneak finds creative ways to defy conventional limitations when trying to overcome extraplanar obstacles.

::ab[Planar Sense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A planar sneak gains a bonus on saving throws against all effects with the air, chaos, earth, evil, fire, good, law, or water descriptors. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Elemental Execution (Su)]{icon=boost l4="A planar sneak treats creatures with the elemental subtype as though they were vulnerable to her sneak attack, but she deals only half as much sneak attack damage as she normally would. She can confirm critical hits against such creatures, though 50% of the time, the critical hit is automatically negated and damage is instead rolled normally." l8="This ability also applies to all outsiders otherwise immune to critical hits and sneak attack damage." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Guileful polyglot~strong stroke~terrain mastery"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Defensive roll~hide in plain sight~skill mastery"}

poisoner.name: Poisoner
description: ## Poisoner
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/134›

Some consider poisoning an evil act. The poisoner knows poison is just a tool toward an end, and is no different than any other weapon. Some poisoners see themselves as great equalizers, as they are able to craft weapons that the weakest of creatures can wield to devastating effect, but most have no lofty delusions about their work.
::ab[Poison Use (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def ability="A poisoner is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison herself when applying poison to a blade." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Master Poisoner (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A poisoner can use Craft (alchemy) to change the type of a ‹rule/poison›. This requires 1 hour of work with an ‹eq-misc/alchemist's lab› and a Craft (alchemy) skill check with a DC equal to the poison's DC. If successful, the poison's type changes to contact, ingested, inhaled, or injury. If the check fails, the poison is ruined." order=ap passive="The poisoner also receives a bonus on Craft (alchemy) skill checks when working with poison equal to 1/2 her rogue level." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Distracting attack~lasting poison~surprise attack~swift poison~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deadly cocktail~hunter's surprise~opportunist"}

rake.name: Rake
description: ## Rake
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/134›

The rake is a rogue who is open about her skills and talents, often to the point of being boastful. Usually she has the protection of an important figure who finds her services useful, but sometimes her bravado is enough to keep enemies away. She is often used as a face for the group for diplomacy, gathering information, negotiations, or to gain the most lucrative contracts and quests from local authorities.

::ab[Bravado's Blade (Ex)]{icon=power ability="When a rake hits an opponent and deals sneak attack damage, she can forgo 1d6 points of that damage and make a free ‹skill/Intimidate› check to demoralize the foe. For every additional 1d6 points of sneak attack damage she forgoes, she receives a +5 circumstance bonus on this check." replace=Trapfinding}
::ab[Rake's Smile (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A rake gains a morale bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Befuddling strike~charmer~honeyed words~surprise attack"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~redirect attack~skill mastery"}

relic_raider.name: Relic Raider
description: ## Relic Raider
‹SOURCE Black Markets/27›

Relic raiders are rogues who specialize in recovering treasures from ruins and tombs, some in a quest for knowledge, others out of a desire for riches. In addition to natural dangers and mundane traps, these locations are often protected by ancient curses or the psychic imprints of former occupants. Though not versed in magic, relic raiders learn to unweave these maledictions as easily as they unlock ancient vaults.

::ab[Curse Sense (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A relic raider adds 1/2 her rogue level on Perception checks to notice haunts and on Spellcraft checks to identify cursed items (using ‹spell/detect magic› or similar effects)." order=ap passive="In addition, the relic raider gains a bonus on saving throws against curses and haunts and a dodge bonus to AC against attacks by haunts. @B-3fp-rogue." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Disable Curse (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A relic raider can attempt to disable a curse as she would a magical trap. This ability acts as ‹spell/remove curse›, except instead of a caster level check, the relic raider attempts a Disable Device check (DC = 15 + the DC of the curse). Disabling a curse takes 2d4 rounds, and the relic raider cannot take 10 or 20 on this check." useInc=rogue~4~8 useF="8~1~4" replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Advanced Rogue Talents]{head icon=power ability="A relic raider can select the following new ‹ability/advanced rogue talents›. These talents are considered Primary Sneak Attack Talents, and add effects to a rogue's sneak attack. Only one Primary Sneak Attack Talent can be applied to an individual attack, and the decision must be made before making the attack roll."}

::ab[Cursed Strike (Su)]{next icon=magic prereq="‹talent/Major magic› rogue talent" ability="Once per day, when the relic raider would normally deal sneak attack damage, she can instead forgo all bonus damage to attempt to curse her target (as per the spell ‹spell/bestow curse›). The save is DC equal to 10 + 1/2 the relic raider's rogue level + her Intelligence modifier." imp15="She can use this twice a day." imp20="She can use this three times per day."}

::ab[Etheric Strike (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="With a successful sneak attack against haunts or undead, the relic raider can deal her sneak attack damage as positive energy damage rather than physical damage. The additional damage affects undead that are normally immune to sneak attacks because of their lack of a discernible anatomy (such as ‹template/ghost«s›)." useMod=Charisma3}

river_rat.name: River Rat
description: ## River Rat
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/76;People of the River/21›

What highway robbers are to roads, river rats are to waterways. Skilled at hiding amid reeds and roots, these rogues strike the unwary from the shallows as others would from the shadows. Despite their strong association with banditry, river rats make excellent guides and hunters.
::cskill{gain=kna lose=kd}

::ab[Swamper (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A river rat gains a bonus equal to half her rogue level on Swim checks (minimum +1). She takes no penalty on Acrobatics or Stealth checks for being in bogs and undergrowth." ability="A river rat ignores ‹rule/difficult terrain› caused by light undergrowth and shallow bogs, and it costs her only 2 squares of movement to enter a square of ‹rule/deep bog› or ‹rule/heavy undergrowth›, rather than 4 squares of movement." order=pa special="All of these abilities apply only when she is wearing light or no armor and carrying no more than a light load." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Rat's Resilience (Ex)]{flavor="A river rat is inured to dangers presented by disease-carrying parasites, poisonous fish, and other toxic river denizens." icon=def passive="A river rat gains a bonus on saving throws against disease and poison effects. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~hold breath~rogue crawl~stand up~strong stroke~survivalist"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~opportunist~skill mastery~stealthy sniper"}

roof_runner.name: Roof Runner
description: ## Roof Runner
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/74›

A specialized urban acrobat, the roof runner makes her home high atop the spires and gables of great cities. She is skilled at traveling these uneven surfaces at full speed, performing daring leaps and deftly balancing upon narrow and precarious ledges.

::ab[Roof Running (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power flavor="A roof runner becomes entirely adept at moving across the tops of buildings, spires, and similar locations." ability="Provided she is wearing light armor or no armor, the roof runner can move at full speed while traveling across the tops of buildings or similar structures, and takes no penalties on any Dexterity-based Skill checks or Reflex saves that might be incurred from moving about on a roof." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Tumbling Descent (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A roof runner can use her Acrobatics skill to attempt a rapid descent from a rooftop or another surface, ricocheting against another surface and then diving through an opening (such as a balcony or window) directly below. So long as she has at least two surfaces no farther than 10 feet apart to bounce against, she can ricochet her body back from one to the next, descending great distances with a single check. The DC is 10 + 5 for every additional 10-foot increment descended beyond the initial 10 feet dropped. If she fails, she falls the full distance." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Expert leaper~ledge walker~nimble climber~stand up~terrain mastery"}
@HL[This advanced rogue talent complements this archetype:] ‹talent/Fast tumble›

rotdrinker.name: Rotdrinker
description: ## Rotdrinker
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/12›

Poisons are common tools in a rogue's trade, and some poison-using rogues are so frequently exposed to these toxins that they have developed resistances to particular poisons. Known as rotdrinkers, these rogues purposefully consume poisons to augment their minds and bodies, though they do so at the expense of traditional roguish nimbleness. This archetype is appropriate for rogues built using the Core Rulebook as well for unchained rogues.
::ab[Poison Resistance (Ex)]{icon=boost l2="A rotdrinker gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against all poisons." l8="This bonus increases to +4." replace=Evasion}

:::ab{title="Unnatural Ingestion (Ex)" action="Ability" l=4 icon=def-aid-boost order=pC passive="A rotdrinker gains immunity to all ingested poisons, including poisons that are converted to or considered ingested poisons (such as a poison converted to an ingested poison using the alchemist's poison conversion discovery)." replace="Improved uncanny dodge~uncanny dodge" imp8="The temporary hit points and additional benefits last for a number of minutes equal to the rotdrinker's rogue level."}

Further, when the rotdrinker ingests an ingested poison, she gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to half her rogue level. These temporary hit points stack with temporary hit points from other effects, and they last until they are lost or for a number of rounds equal to the rotdrinker's rogue level, whichever comes first.

Additionally, the rotdrinker gains benefits from the ingested poisons that she ingests depending on the poison's details, as explained below. A rotdrinker can gain hit points and additional benefits from only one ingested poison at a time. If she ingests another poison while she has temporary hit points and additional benefits from another poison, the previous temporary hit points and benefits are lost.
Ingested poisons provide an alchemical bonus equal to the number of consecutive saving throws required to cure the poison (for example, ‹eq-poison/lich dust› provides a +2 bonus).
- An ingested poison that affects Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution grants this bonus on the rotdrinker's damage rolls and Fortitude saving throws.
- An ingested poison that affects Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma instead grants this bonus on the rotdrinker's attack rolls and Will saving throws.
- An ingested poison that does not affect any ability scores (such as ‹eq-poison/oil of taggit›) grants this bonus to the rotdrinker's AC.
If an ingested poison falls into more than one of these categories, the rotdrinker chooses which benefit she receives at the time of ingestion.

sanctified_rogue.name: Sanctified Rogue
description: ## Sanctified Rogue
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/74›

The sanctified rogue serves a higher purpose, acting as a representative of a church or cult, or following the tenets of a specific faith or deity. While most sanctified rogues share the beliefs or ideals of the religions they represent, not all of them are necessarily pious. A handful of disreputable holy thieves serve purely as mercenaries. At the other end of spectrum, however, more sanctified rogues adhere to the tenets of their faith with fanatical devotion. Regardless, all seem blessed with the ability to call upon divine favor to aid them in times of need.

::ab[Divine Purpose (Su)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="The favor of a deity or religious institution grants a special blessing on a sanctified rogue, shoring up some of her weaknesses." passive="She gains a +1 sacred bonus on Fortitude and Will saving throws." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Divine Epiphany (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="Once per day, a sanctified rogue can peer into the immediate future and predict the results of a specific action as if she had cast an ‹spell/augury› spell. The caster level of this spell is equal to the sanctified rogue's rogue class level." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Esoteric scholar~major magic~minor magic"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Feat~skill mastery"}

sapper.name: Sapper
description: ## Sapper
‹SOURCE Dungeoneer's Handbook/21›
The sapper is a trained siege engineer and can deal with traps and other obstacles given plenty of time.

::ab[Destructive Dismantle (Ex)]{flavor="A sapper is able to quickly breach obstacles." icon=power ability="Using a combination of engineering, explosives, alchemical reagents, and sheer bloody-mindedness, she can deal up to 10 points of damage per sapper level upon an object, ignoring the object's hardness. She might crack open a stone wall with an explosive charge, or collapse a ceiling by tunneling through its supports." usage="Once a day" special="This ability requires 1d4+1 hours of preparation, so it can be used only on immobile and unresisting objects." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Sapping (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A sapper gains a +4 bonus on Perception or Disable Device checks to find or disable traps." order=pa ability="She also grants a +4 bonus when using the ‹rule/aid another› action to assist with ‹rule/Strength› checks to lift gates, bend bars, or force open doors. However, the sapper is focused, so it takes her a @HLfull-round_action to complete any aid another action." replace="2nd-level rogue talent"}

::ab[Fence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A sapper loots a dungeon or other adventuring complex of its mundane goods and sells them to her various contacts. These mundane goods include things like brass fittings, stewpots, scrap metal, and so on. The sapper automatically loots this junk while in the dungeon, and must spend 24 uninterrupted hours selling the objects in town. She shares a portion of the proceeds with the adventurers; the amount typically equals 1d10 gp per sapper level per dungeon." replace="4th-level rogue talent"}

scout.name: Scout
description: ## Scout
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/134›

Not all rogues live in the city. Scouts frequently roam the wilderness, often banding together as bandits, but sometimes serving as guides, as trailblazers, or as companions to a ranger or barbarian warrior. More comfortable with sneaking and hiding outdoors, the scout is still effective in the city and the dungeon.

::ab[Scout's Charge (Ex)]{l=4 icon=melee ability="Whenever a scout makes a ‹rule/charge›, her attack deals sneak attack damage as if the target were ‹rule/flat-footed›. Foes with *uncanny dodge* are immune to this ability." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Skirmisher (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="Whenever a scout moves more than 10 feet in a round and makes an attack action, the attack deals sneak attack damage as if the target was flat-footed. If the scout makes more than one attack this turn, this ability only applies to the first attack. Foes with *uncanny dodge* are immune to this ability." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Assault leader~camouflage~cunning trigger~survivalist~trap spotter"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hunter's surprise~skill mastery~stealthy sniper"}

scroll_scoundrel.name: Scroll Scoundrel
description: ## Scroll Scoundrel
‹SOURCE Pathfinder Society Field Guide/27›

The scroll scoundrel is a rogue archetype that relies on cunning and quick wits to stay alive. He excels in exploiting overconfidence and predictability, using knowledge he's gained from his extensive research and field experience combined with hearsay and recent observations to react to problems as soon as they arise with swift but unnaturally accurate guesswork. A scroll scoundrel is often called upon by the Pathfinders to undertake missions where killing a foe isn't nearly as important as humiliating them in public duels or similar conflicts. A scroll scoundrel has the following class features.

::ab[Fast Talker (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A scroll scoundrel's penalty on Bluff checks that are unlikely, farfetched, or impossible to achieve is reduced by 5. When he makes a Diplomacy check to adjust a creature's starting attitude, he gains a +3 competence bonus on the skill check." replace="2nd-level rogue talent"}

::ab[Adaptive Learning (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A scroll scoundrel gains a bonus on saving throws against abilities and spells he has already successfully saved against within the past minute (even if the spells or abilities in question were used by different creatures). @B-3m-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Pay it Back (Ex)]{icon=boost l4="A scroll scoundrel receives a +1 bonus on attack rolls and weapon damage rolls against foes who made melee attacks against him in the previous round." l10="This bonus increases to +2." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Elusive Gambit (Ex)]{l=6 icon=melee flavor="A scroll scoundrel's movement can leave a foe off-balance." ability="If a scroll scoundrel provokes an attack of opportunity by moving past an opponent and that opponent then misses with the attack, that foe is considered to be ‹rule/flat-footed› against the scroll scoundrel's next melee attack, provided that melee attack comes within 1 round of the missed attack of opportunity." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Spot Weakness (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost swift="A scroll scoundrel can discover a weakness in his enemy's defenses. His next successful hit in the same round ignores 5 points of the foe's damage reduction or hardness." imp15="He ignores the first 15 points of DR or hardness." imp20="He ignores the first 20 points of DR or hardness." special="If the attack deals any sneak attack damage, he ignores all of the foe's damage reduction or hardness for that attack. He cannot use this ability on foes that are immune to critical hits." replace="10th-level rogue talent"}

sczarni_swindler.name: Sczarni Swindler
description: ## Sczarni Swindler
‹SOURCE The Harrow Handbook/13›
If life is a game, the Sczarni play to win. When skill and luck aren't enough, cheating is often the best option.

::ab[Let Fate Decide (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost-down ability="The Sczarni swindler can declare two different actions that rely on different types of rolls or checks, such as attacking a creature (an attack roll) or sneaking past the same creature (a Stealth check). As a @HLstandard_action, the swindler then uses a random method to choose one of the declared activities, such as flipping a coin, rolling a die, or drawing a harrow card. The specific method doesn't matter as long as there is an equal chance of either activity being chosen. If the swindler then performs the chosen activity within the next round, she gains a luck bonus on the roll type required for that activity - attack rolls with a specific weapon, a specific skill check, a specific ability check, or a specific saving throw - equal to half her rogue level (minimum +1) for 1 minute." special="If the swindler performs any other action (whether declared or not) in the round after using this ability, she becomes ‹misc/shaken› for 1 minute instead." useMod=Charisma3 replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Quicker Than the Eye (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power ability="When the Sczarni swindler uses Sleight of Hand, creatures take a penalty on their Perception checks equal to half the swindler's class level to notice the attempt. The swindler also reduces the normal -20 penalty by an amount equal to her class level when attempting a ‹skill/Sleight of Hand› check as a move action instead of as a standard action. Lastly, the swindler can draw hidden weapons or objects from her person as a @HLmove_action instead of the usual standard action." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Poker Face (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-power passive="The Sczarni swindler gains a bonus on Bluff, Profession (gambler), and Sense Motive checks equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level." ability="The Sczarni swindler can also attempt to feint against non-humanoid targets without penalty, though she still takes a -8 penalty on feint attempts against creatures with animal intelligence and she cannot feint against mindless creatures." order=pa replace="Trap sense"}
::ab[No Fool (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="The Sczarni swindler gains a bonus on Will saving throws equal to one-fourth of her rogue level." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Cheat Fate (Ex)]{icon=roll l8="Once per day, the Sczarni swindler can reroll any one d20 roll she has just made before the GM has revealed the result. She must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse." l14="The swindler can use this ability twice per day." l20="She can use this ability three times per day." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Canny observer~fast fingers~hard to fool~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Master of disguise~skill mastery~slippery mind"}

seeker_of_the_lost.name: Seeker of the Lost
description: ## Seeker of the Lost
‹SOURCE Blood of the Sea/5›

Aquatic elven adventurers descending into the ruins of sunken Azlant developed techniques to ease their explorations, and they now share these techniques among a loose group of acquaintances calling themselves the seekers of the lost. These aquatic elves eagerly explore trap-laden ruins of all types, using their skills to bypass traps and dispatch animated guardians put in place long ago by the lost cities' original founders. These seekers rarely travel far inland, though they may journey to larger landlocked bodies of water, such as Lake Encarthan, to explore submerged ruins there.

::ab[Arcana Breaker (Su)]{flavor="Typically, only magical traps survive for long periods underwater, so a seeker of the lost specializes in noticing and disarming such traps." icon=boost l3="A seeker of the lost gains a competence bonus on Perception checks to notice magical traps and on Disable Device checks to disarm magical traps. @B-3f-rogue." replace="2nd-level rogue talent"}

::ab[Underwater Striker (Ex)]{l=4 icon=melee flavor="A seeker of the lost gains the ability to strike precisely underwater." ability="When a seeker of the lost makes a sneak attack with a melee weapon, the damage is not halved for ‹rule/physical_attacks>being made» underwater›, as is usually the case for attacks that deal slashing or bludgeoning damage or, if the rogue is ‹rule/off-balance›, attacks that deal piercing damage." special="In addition, when a seeker of the lost makes a sneak attack with a melee weapon against a construct, she ignores any hardness the construct has." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Wary Disarm (Ex)]{l=8 icon=def ability="Whenever a seeker of the lost attempts to disarm a trap using Disable Device, she does not spring the trap unless she fails by an amount equal to her rogue level or more." special="If she does set off a trap she was attempting to disarm, she doubles her *trap sense* bonus to avoid the trap." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Canny observer~strong stroke~terrain mastery|water~trap spotter"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Defensive roll~dispelling attack~hide in plain sight|water"}

shadow_scion.name: Shadow Scion
description: ## Shadow Scion
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/15›

Count Ranalc's worshipers congregate in the places between dark and light, searching for secrets that might one day free their patron from his mysterious exile. Shadow scions have learned some of these secrets and draw power from the shadows themselves. Many shadow scions are dedicated ‹race/fetchling› or ‹race/wayang› devotees of ‹faith/Count Ranalc›.

::ab[Shadow Dweller (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l1="A shadow scion gains darkvision with a range of 30 feet and a +1 competence bonus on Stealth checks in ‹rule/dim light› and darkness. If she already has darkvision, the range of her darkvision increases by 10 feet." increment="Her darkvision's range increases by 10 feet, and her Stealth bonus~1~2" replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Shadow Strike]{icon=power l2="A shadow scion gains ‹feat/Shadow Strike› as a bonus feat. A character using the unchained rogue class instead gains ‹feat/Blind-Fight› as a bonus feat." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Shadow Step (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="A shadow scion can walk through the Shadow Plane and reappear elsewhere on the Material Plane. The shadow scion can travel up to 30 feet per rogue level per day in this fashion, either in a single round or broken up across multiple shadow steps. This movement must be used in increments of at least 5 feet and does not provoke attacks of opportunity." special="Travel through the Shadow Plane is imprecise; when the shadow scion arrives back on the Material Plane, she reenters 1 square off target, as per the rules for ‹rule/throw«n» splash weapon«s›. If this would place her in an occupied square, she instead arrives in the nearest safe location. When the shadow scion arrives on the Material Plane, she is cloaked in shadow and gains ‹rule/concealment› as per the ‹spell/blur› spell for 1 round." replace="Rogue talent gained at 8th level"}

::ab[Shadow Speaker (Sp)]{l=14 icon=magic ability="Once per day, a shadow scion can spend 1 minute meditating within a patch of dim light or darkness; at the end of the time, she can draw information from the shadows. This functions as ‹spell/commune with nature›, except that it functions in any terrain to a radius of 100 feet per rogue level, and it cannot reveal information about any object or subject that is not currently in dim light or darkness." imp19="The shadow scion can use this ability twice per day." replace="Rogue talent gained at 14th level"}

::ab[Shadow Master (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-lower passive="Whenever a shadow scion is in an area of dim light, she gains DR 10/cold iron and a +2 luck bonus on all saving throws." order=pa ability="In addition, whenever she confirms a critical hit against a foe that is in an area of dim light, that foe is ‹misc/blinded› for 1d6 rounds." replace="Master strike"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="False friend~fast stealth~slow reactions~surprise attack~underhanded"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~improved evasion~slippery mind~unwitting ally"}

shadow_walker.name: Shadow Walker
description: ## Shadow Walker
‹SOURCE Blood of Shadows/9›

Shadow walkers are comfortable in light, darkness, and the shadows in between. Fetchling rogues naturally gravitate toward becoming shadow walkers, but shadow walkers exist among other creatures both above and below the surface.

::ab[Expanded Sight (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-def passive="A shadow walker gains darkvision with a range of 30 feet. If she already has darkvision, the range of her darkvision increases by 10 feet." repeat="The range of her darkvision increases by 10 feet.~3~2" imp3="The shadow walker loses the ‹umr/light sensitivity› weakness, if she has it. If she has ‹umr/light blindness›, she instead treats it as light sensitivity." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Illumination Control (Sp)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="A shadow walker can manipulate nearby illumination." ability="At the start of each day, a shadow walker gains a number of illumination points equal to half her rogue level and can spend illumination points to use certain spell-like abilities. As long as she has at least 1 illumination point, she can cast ‹spell/light› at will. She can spend 2 illumination points to cast ‹spell/darkness›, and 3 illumination points to cast ‹spell/daylight›, ‹spell/deeper darkness›, or ‹spell/motes of dusk and dawn›." special="These spell-like abilities have a caster level equal to the shadow walker's rogue level. Using these spell-like abilities does not hamper the shadow walker's vision; for example, she can see through the *deeper darkness* she creates, and does not take penalties for light sensitivity in the area of her own *daylight.*" replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level~trap sense"}

::ab[Favored Illumination (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost choice="A shadow walker chooses one ‹rule/illumination level›: bright light, normal light, dim light, or darkness (including supernatural darkness)." order=ca ability="While she is within her chosen illumination level, she gains a bonus (equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level) on initiative checks and Acrobatics, Perception, and Sleight of Hand skill checks, and can take 10 on any Stealth check attempted within that illumination level." special="When the shadow walker confirms a critical hit with a melee attack that deals sneak attack damage while in her chosen illumination level, she regains 1 illumination point (to a maximum of half her rogue level). Confirming a critical hit on a creature that has fewer Hit Dice than half the shadow walker's character level doesn't restore illumination points." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deft palm~gloom magic~greater gloom magic~obscuring blow~surprise attack~umbral gear"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hide in plain sight~see in darkness~slippery mind~unwitting ally~weapon snatcher"}

sharper.name: Sharper
description: ## Sharper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/47›

Sharpers use trickery and deception to part an owner from his valuables. Pathological risk-takers, they distinguish themselves from con artists and burglars by virtue of the simplicity and boldness of their schemes, which are usually impromptu, rather than carefully orchestrated. Many sharpers consider themselves romantic figures and foster the image of a resourceful, dashing rogue living by wits and luck while subverting authority at every turn.
::ab[Scam Artist (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A sharper gains a bonus equal to half her rogue level (minimum +1) on all Bluff and Sleight of Hand checks." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Sticky Fingers (Ex)]{icon=power l2="A sharper receives ‹feat/Improved Steal› as a bonus feat." l6="She receives ‹feat/Greater Steal› as a bonus feat." imp8="She receives ‹feat/Quick Steal› as a bonus feat." special="The sharper gains these feats even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." replace="Rogue talents gained at 2nd, 6th, and 8th levels"}

::ab[Lucky Save (Su)]{icon=def flavor="When a sharper's wits aren't enough to pull her out of a bad situation, her luck still just might save her." l3="She gains a +1 luck bonus on all saving throws." increment="This bonus~3~6" replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Audacious Overconfidence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=roll-def ability="After failing an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw, a sharper can reduce her saving throw bonus from *lucky save* by 1 for 24 hours in order to reroll the failed roll." useInc=rogue~6~4 useF="4~1~6" special="The decreases to her bonus from *lucky save* stack with each other." replace="Rogue talents gained at 4th, 10th, and 16th levels"}

::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Certainty|unchained rogue only~charmer~convincing lie~deft palm~false friend~fast fingers~follow along~honeyed words~obfuscate story~shades of gray~steal the story"}

::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~cutting edge|unchained rogue only~defensive roll~hidden mind~improved evasion~redirect attack~skill mastery"}

skulking_slayer.name: Skulking Slayer
description: ## Skulking Slayer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/55›

Pushed into a life of crime by the society around them, half-orcs gravitate toward criminal activities that suit them best. Half-orc rogues leave subtle tactics and finesse to halflings and elves, and rely on brute strength and thuggery when they go about making mischief. Skulking slayers have turned the use of raw strength and surprise into an art form. A skulking slayer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Half-Orc}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="The skulking slayer gains proficiency with ‹eq-weapon/greatclub«s› and ‹eq-weapon/whip«s›, but loses proficiency with rapiers and hand crossbows."}
::cskill{lose=ddev~ling~soh}
::ab[Skill Ranks per Level]{icon=down note="She gains 6 + Int modifier skill points each level."}

::ab[Pass for Human (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="When trying to conceal her half-orc heritage, a skulking slayer gains a bonus on Disguise checks equal to ‹misc/half› her level. When using disguise to appear as a specific individual, skulking stalkers ignore the normal -2 penalty to appear as another race."}

::ab[Underhanded Maneuvers (Ex)]{l=1 icon=melee ability="When she could normally make a sneak attack, a slayer may instead make a ‹rule/dirty trick› or ‹rule/steal› combat maneuver with a bonus on her roll. This bonus is equal to her number of sneak attack dice for a dirty trick combat maneuver, or 1-1/2 &times& her number of sneak attack dice for a steal combat maneuver." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l2="A skulking slayer can select the ‹feat/Surprise Follow-Through› feat in place of a rogue talent." l10="She can select the ‹feat/Improved Surprise Follow-Through› feat (if she already has Surprise Follow-Through) in place of an advanced rogue talent."}

::ab[Bold Strike (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="When a skulking slayer ‹rule/charge«s› and makes a sneak attack with a two-handed weapon, she rolls d8s instead of d6s for her sneak attack damage." replace="Trap sense +1 and +4"}
::ab[Shifty (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="A skulking slayer gains a bonus on Bluff checks to feint equal to half her level." replace="Trap sense +2"}
::ab[Unexpected Charge (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power swift="A skulking slayer can make a ‹skill/Bluff› check to feint before a charge." replace="Trap sense +3"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Combat swipe~combat trick~powerful sneak~surprise attack~terrain mastery"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~deadly sneak~unwitting ally"}

sly_saboteur.name: Sly Saboteur
description: ## Sly Saboteur
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/77›

Sly saboteurs are irregular soldiers who specialize in sabotage and surprise attacks. Though they often face enemies with greater numbers or resources (or both), sly saboteurs have the advantage of knowing the local people and terrain. They might be rebels fighting against a corrupt government, or they might be members of a militia opposing an invading force. Some are environmentalists who sabotage the tools of industry in order to protect unspoiled nature.
::cskill{gain=sur lose=kd}

::ab[Scamper (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A sly saboteur can move through 5 feet of ‹rule/difficult terrain› each round as if it were normal terrain." incrementPlain="This distance~3~3~10~5" incrementEnd=" feet." special="This ability allows the sly saboteur to take a 5-foot step into difficult terrain. This ability applies only when she is wearing light armor or no armor and carrying no more than a light load, and it stacks with the ‹feat/Acrobatic Steps› and ‹feat/Nimble Moves› feats." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Catastrophic Failure (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When a sly saboteur uses Disable Device to rig a device to fail, she can increase the DC of the check by 5. If she does so and succeeds at the check, when the device fails, users take damage equal to the sly saboteur's sneak attack, or they take half that amount with a successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + half the sly saboteur's level + her Dexterity modifier)." special="The type of damage depends on the nature of the device but is typically bludgeoning, fire, piercing, or slashing." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Disable Magic Device (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A sly saboteur can use Disable Device to rig a magic item to fail as if it were an extremely complex device. If the item's effect is instantaneous, the effect is countered the next time the item is activated, but the activation still consumes a charge or use. If the item's effect is continuous or has a duration, the effect fails after 1d4 rounds or minutes of use (sly saboteur's choice). This ends the duration or suppresses a continuous effect for 1d4 rounds (as the spell ‹spell/dispel magic›). This ability cannot be used on artifacts." special="If this ability is combined with the *catastrophic failure* ability, the resulting damage is untyped magic and is not reduced by damage reduction or energy resistance." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Cunning trigger~fast stealth~getaway artist~quick disable~quick trapsmith~rogue crawl~trap spotter~wall scramble"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Frugal trapsmith~hide in plain sight~knock-out blow~rumormonger~skill mastery~stealthy sniper"}

smuggler.name: Smuggler
description: ## Smuggler
‹SOURCE Pirates of the Inner Sea/23›

Smugglers specialize in moving contraband from one area to another while avoiding detection by authorities. Sometimes a smuggler's cargo is illegal goods, such as drugs or slaves. Other times, a smuggler hides legal goods to avoid paying duties or taxes, and some may even use their abilities to help smuggle escaped slaves to freedom. Smugglers may work on a ship that does nothing but smuggle goods from one port to another, or they may freelance, working on ships that require a smuggler's services for a short time.

::ab[Conceal Item]{icon=boost-power passive="A smuggler adds 1/2 her level on Sleight of Hand checks (minimum +1)." ability="A smuggler can use Sleight of Hand to conceal a small item in a container or room, such as the false bottom of a chest or beneath floorboards. Anyone attempting to find an item concealed by a smuggler makes a Perception check opposed by the Sleight of Hand check made by the smuggler when she concealed the object." order=pa replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Distraction]{flavor="A smuggler uses patter and body language to deflect searchers from hidden items." l2="A smuggler may make a Bluff check opposed by a searcher's Perception check when a searcher looks for items the smuggler has concealed. If the smuggler's Bluff check succeeds, the searcher must make a second Perception check and take the lower of the two rolls." icon=roll replace=Evasion}

::ab[Bribery]{icon=power flavor="An experienced smuggler knows how to smooth over interactions with customs officials." l3="When dealing with officials working at a checkpoint, conducting an inspection, or performing another such activity, a smuggler can make a Diplomacy check opposed by the presiding official's Sense Motive check to ensure her contraband makes it through. She gains a bonus on this Diplomacy check equal to ‹misc/one-third› of her rogue level. On a successful check, the smuggler pays a small bribe (5d4 gp) to guarantee her cargo remains undisturbed." replace="Trap sense"}

snare_setter.name: Snare Setter
description: ## Snare Setter
‹SOURCE Kobolds of Golarion/23›
Deep in darkness, the snare setter constructs his deadly traps and brutal mechanisms to confound and destroy any who try to invade his domain.
::prereq{r=Kobold}

::ab[Trapper (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A snare setter gains ‹feat/Learn Ranger Trap› as a bonus feat. In addition to the trap granted by this feat, he learns how to construct the ‹rangertrap/tripwire› trap for free. For the purposes of this feat, he's considered a ranger of his snare setter level, and uses his Intelligence in place of Wisdom to determine uses per day and the DC of his ranger traps." special="Whenever the snare setter could learn a new rogue trick, he may instead learn a new ranger trap." replace="Sneak attack (but see below)"}

::ab[Trapsmithing (Ex)]{icon=boost-power ability="A snare setter gains a bonus on Perception skill checks to detect traps and on Craft (traps) checks equal to 1/2 his snare setter level. He can use Craft (traps) in place of Disable Device to disarm traps, including magical traps." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Deadly Traps (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The snare setter's traps deal an additional 1d6 points of damage on the first round they're activated. This damage is of the same type the trap normally deals. Traps that don't deal damage are unaffected by this ability." incrementPlain="This additional damage~3~4" incrementEnd="d6."}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Su)]{l=5 icon=power ability="The snare setter gains ‹ability/sneak attack› +1d6. This functions as the rogue ability of the same name." increment="The sneak attack damage~5~4" incrementEnd="d6."}

::ab[Master Snare Setter (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="The snare setter can attach one of a rogue's *master strike* effects to a trap he creates. When the trap is triggered, the subject makes an additional Fortitude save at the same DC as the trap to avoid this effect. A snare setter can have only one trap affected by this ability active at any time. He may remove this ability from a trap at any range as a @HLfree_action." replace="Master strike"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~cunning trigger~fast stealth~sniper's eye"}

sniper.name: Sniper
description: ## Sniper
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/134›

Some say that the sniper is the worst kind of assassin: a killer who waits silently in the shadows and then strikes from a distance without remorse. Snipers, of course, understand that such protestations about "cowardice" and "honor" regarding their profession are in fact merely the bleatings of sheep fearing the slaughter and pay them no heed. Most snipers take pride in their formidable abilities, which allow them to take life quickly, quietly, and efficiently, then disappear into their surroundings without a trace.
::ab[Accuracy (Ex)]{l=1 icon=ranged-boost passive="A sniper halves all range increment penalties when making ranged attacks with a bow or crossbow." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Deadly Range (Ex)]{icon=ranged-boost l3="A sniper increases the range at which she can apply her sneak attack damage by 10 feet." incrementPlain="This range~3~3~20~10" incrementEnd=" feet." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~snap shot~sniper's eye~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Defensive roll~master of disguise~stealthy sniper"}

snoop.name: Snoop
description: ## Snoop
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/47›

Snoops are the underworld's version of detectives. They often act as information peddlers, specializing in gathering secrets through investigation, subterfuge, and coercion, and selling or trading those secrets for personal gain.

::ab[Inspiration (Ex)]{l=1 head icon=power ability="A snoop gains an ability similar to the ‹class/investigator«'s› inspiration class ability. The snoop begins with an inspiration pool equal to ‹misc/half› her rogue level plus her Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). Unlike an investigator, a snoop can only use inspiration on skill checks, not on attack rolls or saving throws. This ability is otherwise identical to the investigator class ability of the same name." replace=Trapfinding~evasion}

::ab[Inspiration (Ex)]{next icon=boost order=afu flavor="Text from the investigator class." usage="You can only use inspiration once per check or roll." ability="Your inspiration pool refreshes each day, typically after you get a restful night's sleep." free="You can expend one use of inspiration from your pool to add 1d6 to the result of a check, including any on which you take 10 or 20. This choice is made after the check is rolled and before the results are revealed." special="You can use inspiration on any Knowledge, Linguistics, or Spellcraft skill checks without expending a use of inspiration, if you're trained in the skill."}

::ab[Investigator Talents (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="Each time she selects a new rogue talent, a snoop can instead select one of the following investigator talents: ‹invtalent/eidetic recollection›, ‹invtalent/empathy›, ‹invtalent/hidden agendas›, ‹invtalent/inspired alertness›, ‹invtalent/inspired intimidator›, ‹invtalent/item lore›, or ‹invtalent/underworld inspiration›. Her effective investigator level for the purpose of these talents is equal to her rogue level." special="She still can't use inspiration on attack rolls or saving throws (so, for instance, she can't use the second part of *hidden agendas*)."}

::ab[Uncanny Snoop (Ex)]{icon=boost l4="A snoop gains a +2 bonus on Intimidate checks when trying to force an opponent to give her information (or Bluff and Diplomacy checks for the same purpose if she has the *coax information* rogue talent). She also gains a +2 bonus on Sense Motive checks to tell if someone's information is false." imp8="These bonuses increase to +4." replace="Uncanny dodge~improved uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Master of Whispers (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A snoop receives the ‹talent/rumormonger› advanced rogue talent." replace="Rogue talent gained at 8th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~certainty|unchained rogue only~coax information~fast stealth~follow clues~strong impression"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Cutting edge|unchained rogue only~hard minded~hidden mind~skill mastery~thoughtful reexamining"}

spy.name: Spy
description: ## Spy
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/135›

The best spies are like chameleons, but not only can they change their appearances to fit the situation, they can also change their personalities, allegiances, and even loves if that's what it takes to achieve their clandestine goals. Spies are the ultimate manipulators, and even those who commission their services sometimes find that they've merely served the spies' own interests.

::ab[Skilled Liar (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="Whenever a spy uses ‹skill/Bluff› to attempt to deceive someone, she gains a bonus on the opposed roll equal to 1/2 her rogue level (minimum +1). This bonus does not apply to feint attempts or attempts to pass secret messages." replace=Trapfinding}
::ab[Poison Use (Ex)]{l3="A spy is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison herself when applying poison to a blade." icon=def replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Canny observer~guileful polyglot~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic~quick disguise"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~master of disguise~stealthy sniper"}

survivalist.name: Survivalist
description: ## Survivalist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/74›

The survivalist focuses her talents on surviving harsh and unforgiving conditions that would kill a lesser rogue. Whether in blazing deserts or frigid arctic wastes - or simply the cold, hard reality of the streets - the survivalist uses her training to both ensure her own success and provide for the safety of her allies.

::ab[Hardy (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def ability="A survivalist is already prepared to endure extreme hardships and environmental conditions. She can go twice the normal number of days without water and triple the normal number of days without food before feeling the effects of either thirst or starvation." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Endure Elements (Sp)]{l=3 icon=magic ability="A survivalist rogue can use ‹spell/endure elements› once per day as a spell-like ability. The survivalist's caster level is the same as her rogue level." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Hold breath~iron guts~resiliency~strong stroke~survivalist~terrain mastery~wall scramble"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~defensive roll~feat~skill mastery"}

swamp_poisoner.name: Swamp Poisoner
description: ## Swamp Poisoner
‹SOURCE Potions and Poisons/4›

Swamp poisoners use their own toxins to defend their homes and level the playing field. The swamp poisoner is an archetype available to grippli characters, and it is appropriate for rogues built using the rules from the Pathfinder RPG Core Rulebook as well as for unchained rogues.
::prereq{r=Grippli other="*Toxic skin* alternate racial trait"}

::ab[Mucous Membrane (Ex)]{icon=boost-def passive="A swamp poisoner gains a bonus equal to half his class level on Escape Artist checks and to his CMD when resisting grapple attempts (minimum 1)." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Mark the Vein (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower ability="When the swamp poisoner attacks a target that is currently affected by his grippli racial poison, that target is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC. The target must be actively poisoned, not just suffering the effects of poison, such as lingering ability damage." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Poison the Well (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power standard="A swamp poisoner can smear the poison generated through the use of his toxic skin alternate racial trait onto the ground in a 5-foot area, instead of onto a weapon or his own body. The first creature other than the swamp poisoner to enter the area is exposed to the poison. The poison loses its potency after 1 hour. This area is considered a ‹rule/trap<s›, and the DCs of Perception checks to notice it and Disable Device checks to disable it are equal to 10 + half the swamp poisoner's level + the swamp poisoner's Constitution modifier." order=sa ability="The swamp poisoner also gains extra uses per day of his toxic skin alternate racial trait equal to ‹misc/one-third› of his rogue level." replace="Trap sense (core rogue)~danger sense (unchained rogue)"}

swashbuckler.name: Swashbuckler
description: ## Swashbuckler
>***Note:** This is a rogue archetype, and has no relation to the ‹class/swashbuckler› hybrid class.*
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/135›

A paragon of mobile swordplay, the swashbuckler is a rogue who focuses almost exclusively on honing her skill at arms and perfecting daring acrobatic moves and elaborate flourishes that border on performance.

::ab[Martial Training (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="The swashbuckler may select one martial weapon to add to her list of weapon proficiencies. In addition, she may take the ‹talent/combat trick› rogue talent up to two times." replace=Trapfinding}
::ab[Daring (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A swashbuckler gains a morale bonus on Acrobatics checks and saving throws against fear. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Offensive defense~positioning attack~powerful sneak~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~entanglement of blades~redirect attack"}

swordmaster.name: Swordmaster
description: ## Swordmaster
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/164›
A swordmaster meditates to strengthen her spiritual connection to her blade. She strives to perfect her skills by mastering six deadly trances. A swordmaster has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Tengu}
::cskill{gain=kna~sur lose=dis~kd}

::ab[Trance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power order=nrc note="A swordmaster learns to focus her martial prowess using an intense meditative trance. Under the influence of a trance, the swordmaster can perform fantastic martial feats." fullround="Entering a trance provokes attacks of opportunity. The swordmaster can maintain the trance for a number of rounds per day equal to 1 + her rogue level + her Wisdom modifier. She can end her trance as a @HLfree_action." choice="The swordmaster chooses one trance from the list below." special="The swordmaster can only use one type of trance at a time. Following a trance, the swordmaster is ‹misc/fatigued› for a number of rounds equal to 2 &times& the number of rounds she spent in the trance. A swordmaster cannot enter a new trance while fatigued but can otherwise enter a trance multiple times during a single encounter or combat. If a swordmaster falls unconscious, her trance immediately ends." replace="Trap sense" repeat="She chooses another trance from the list below.~6~3"}

::ab[Crane Trance (Ex)]{next icon=power flavor="The swordmaster's blade rises and falls with the graceful sweeping arcs of the mountain crane." ability="When in this trance, a swordmaster gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Crane Style› feat."}

::ab[Dragon Trance (Ex)]{next icon=power flavor="Like the dragon, the swordmaster has honed the steadiness of her mind and body." ability="When in this trance, a swordmaster gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Dragon Style› feat."}

::ab[Leopard Trance (Ex)]{next icon=power flavor="Using the swiftness of the leopard, a swordmaster's evasive footwork confuses her opponents." ability="When in this trance, a swordmaster gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Mobility› feat."}

::ab[Monkey Trance (Ex)]{next icon=power flavor="As the monkey springs, the swordmaster leaps from the reach of her enemies." ability="While in this trance, a swordmaster can make an Acrobatics check opposed by an opponent's CMD. If she succeeds, she may move 5 feet as a @HLswift_action within the opponent's threatened area; this movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity and does not count as a 5-foot step."}

::ab[Serpent Trance (Ex)]{next icon=boost flavor="Like the serpent, the swordmaster's quick movements allow her to catch her opponent unawares." ability="While in this trance, a swordmaster receives a +4 bonus on Bluff checks made to feint during combat."}

::ab[Tiger Trance (Ex)]{next icon=melee flavor="The swordmaster pounces upon her opponents, striking with the ferocity and brute force of a wild tiger." ability="While in this trance, a swordmaster can make a combat maneuver check against an opponent within ‹rule/charge› range. If she succeeds, she may charge that opponent and make a full attack against that opponent."}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Befuddling strike~combat trick~positioning attack~stand up~surprise attack~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Confounding blades~crippling strike~defensive roll~hunter's surprise~redirect attack"}

sylvan_trickster.name: Sylvan Trickster
description: ## Sylvan Trickster
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/77›

Sylvan tricksters are rogues who model themselves after the mischievous fey of legend. Though not spellcasters, sylvan tricksters learn minor magical abilities reminiscent of those favored by fey creatures. The journey for many sylvan tricksters began in childhood, learning at an elder's knee the stories of the fey and the secrets of how to resist their tricks. Sylvan tricksters often live in areas where the boundary with the First World is thin, and a few have even been there: voluntarily or not.
::cskill{gain=kna lose=kd}
::ab[Languages]{icon=power ability="A sylvan trickster learns Sylvan as a bonus language."}

::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A sylvan trickster can improve an animal's attitude. This ability functions as the ‹class/druid› ability of the ‹ability/same name»<wild_empathy›, using the sylvan trickster's rogue level as her effective druid level." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Fey Tricks]{icon=power ability="A sylvan trickster can select a witch ‹ability/hex<es› in place of a rogue talent." imp10="The sylvan trickster can select a hex or ‹ability/major hex<es› in place of a rogue talent." imp18="A sylvan trickster can select a hex, major hex, or ‹ability/grand hex<es› in place of a rogue talent." special="She cannot select any hex more than once." alter="Rogue talents"}
::ab[Resist Nature's Lure (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def ability="A sylvan trickster gains ‹ability/resist nature's lure› as the druid ability of the same name." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Fey Resistance (Ex)]{icon=def l8="A sylvan trickster gains DR 2/cold iron." incrementPlain="This damage reduction~11~3~4~2" incrementEnd="/cold iron." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~charmer~honeyed words~major magic~minor magic~survivalist~terrain mastery"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Familiar~hide in plain sight"}

thug.name: Thug
description: ## Thug
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/135›

Some criminals steal with finesse, their victims only discovering the crime when the rogue is long gone and the coin already spent. A thug, on the other hand, cares nothing for finesse. Through both threat and violence, the thug gets what she wants by the promise of force, and has no problem making good on that promise as needed.

::ab[Frightening (Ex)]{icon=lower ability="Whenever a thug successfully uses ‹skill/Intimidate› to demoralize a creature, the duration of the ‹misc/shaken› condition is increased by 1 round. In addition, if the target is shaken for 4 or more rounds, the thug can instead decide to make the target ‹misc/frightened› for 1 round." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Brutal Beating (Ex)]{l=3 icon=lower ability="Whenever a thug deals sneak attack damage, she can choose to forgo 1d6 points of sneak attack damage to make the target ‹misc/sickened› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 her rogue level. This ability does not stack with itself - only the most recent duration applies." replace="Trap sense"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Offensive defense~powerful sneak~strong impression~weapon training"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Crippling strike~deadly sneak~knock-out blow"}

tidal_trickster.name: Tidal Trickster
description: ## Tidal Trickster
