‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/55›
Tidal tricksters roam the seas, using their skillful mastery of the currents to move across aquatic battlefields and throw their enemies off-balance.

::ab[Wisdom of the Waves (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A tidal trickster gains a swim speed equal to her unmodified base land speed (or increases her swim speed by 10 feet, if she has a racial swim speed). Instead of the +8 bonus granted by a swim speed, she gains a racial bonus on Swim checks equal to 4 + half her rogue level (if she has a racial swim speed, she uses the better bonus)." passive="She gains a bonus on Bluff checks equal to half her rogue level." order=ap imp3="She gains a bonus on Will saves whenever she is underwater. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trapfinding~trap sense"}

::ab[Liquid Disruption (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-def flavor="A tidal trickster can use her knowledge of the cohesion of liquids to disrupt certain foes." ability="She can deal sneak attack damage to oozes and creatures with the water subtype that are normally immune to ‹misc/precision damage› (such as ‹monster/water elemental«s›), and she ignores a ‹class/hydro»kineticist«'s› chance to negate precision damage from *elemental overflow.*" alter="Sneak attack" replace="Uncanny dodge and the 4th-level~rogue talent"}

::ab[Tidal Distortion (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="While making a full attack underwater, a tidal trickster can give up one attack to create a *tidal distortion.* When she creates a *tidal distortion,* the tidal trickster moves up to 10 feet. If any part of her movement includes a square adjacent to one or more foes, she can choose one of those foes and attempt a Bluff check with a DC of 11 + either the enemy's Sense Motive or Swim modifier, whichever is higher. Her Bluff check is subject to any penalties the attack roll she sacrificed would have taken, and if she trades an iterative attack gained from having a high base attack bonus, she also takes a penalty based on that attack's lower base attack bonus (a -5 penalty for the second iterative attack, a -10 penalty for the third iterative attack, and a -15 penalty for the fourth iterative attack, if she manages to gain one). If she succeeds at the Bluff check, the movement doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity from that foe and the foe becomes ‹rule/off-balance› until its next turn." special="A tidal trickster can give up any number of her attacks in a given full attack to create *tidal distortions,* but she can't give up two consecutive attacks." replace="Improved uncanny dodge and the 8th-level~rogue talent"}

toxic_talon.name: Toxic Talon
description: ## Toxic Talon
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/75›

Twilight Talons might pursue many tasks, from relatively passive information gathering to staging massive coups meant to depose tyrants. A toxic talon, however, is trained and deployed for one very specific purpose: to defeat foes with unusual and potent poisons. After adopting a profession to serve as a cover for his activities, a toxic talon spends resources and time combining easy-to-obtain materials into dangerous toxins to aid in the defense of Andoran. A toxic talon can use rare, complex, slow-release poisons to make a death look like it happened naturally, but sometimes the toxic talon will use specific poisons to cast suspicion on other organizations, stirring internal conflict that other Twilight Talons can use to their advantage.

The use of poisons in the defense of a good nation is controversial, and other branches of the ‹rule/Eagle Knights› generally disavow the existence of toxic talons among their organization. Many influential leaders among the Eagle Knights, particularly in the Steel Falcons, have pushed to have the practices of the toxic talons banned from use, but these efforts have only pushed the toxic talons into further obscurity. Their role is particularly protected by the Twilight Talons, who sometimes go as far as to outright lie to others in the Eagle Knights about the operations of a toxic talon. At other times, a toxic talon may choose to martyr his freedom by publicly accepting blame for a mission that has had unfortunate public repercussions. In such a case, the toxic talon is not abandoned by his kin - instead, he is placed on a fair and public trial whose purpose is as much to appease the masses as it is to determine whether any moral lines were crossed. In cases where such a toxic talon is found guilty, he is generally sentenced to hard labor or prison (in which case he often continues to run missions inside of prisons or other fortifications for the Eagle Knights as best suits his talents) or to exile (in which case his role in serving Andoran shifts to one of clandestine missions in other nations). A toxic talon who martyrs his freedom in this way and is then captured again understands that it's unlikely he'll be afforded the same level of support from the Eagle Knights, and that the organization may well disavow any official knowledge of his actions. To a toxic talon, this is all merely a deeper, more significant level of service to the well-being of the nation of Andoran. By serving as a sort of sineater for the rest of the Twilight Talons (and at times, for the other branches of the Eagle Knights), such selfless characters can ensure that the organization survives scandal and public outcry to carry on their good work. In such cases, while public recognition for their deeds may never manifest, their brothers and sisters never forget the toxic talon's sacrifice.

::ab[Forceful Breath (Ex)]{icon=down-power ability="A toxic talon is proficient with ‹eq-weapon/blowgun«s› instead of hand crossbows. He can use a blowgun without penalty while prone." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Poison Adept (Ex)]{icon=def-power passive="A toxic talon is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying poison to a projectile weapon." move="He can apply poison to a projectile." order=pm special="This counts as the ‹ability/poison use› ability for the purposes of qualifying for feats, prestige classes, and other options." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Toxin Resistance (Ex)]{flavor="A toxic talon's constant exposure to varied poisons grants him increasing resistance to them." icon=def l=3 passive="A toxic talon gains a bonus on saving throws against diseases and poisons, and he gains a bonus on Heal checks to treat diseases and poisons. He also gains a bonus on caster level checks (including when he activates a magic item) to treat or remove poisons and diseases, such as when using ‹spell/remove disease› or ‹spell/neutralize poison›. Finally, he gains a bonus on Perception checks to identify traps that utilize diseases or poisons. @B-3mp-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Catalyst (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A toxic talon can use secret alchemical techniques to enhance a poison as he applies it to a weapon, increasing the save DC of the poison by 1. This increase to the save DC lasts for 1 minute or until the poison is used up, whichever comes first." special="A toxic talon can use *catalyst* once per hour." imp12="The save DC now increases by 2." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Split Toxin (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="When a toxic talon uses *catalyst* to enhance poison as he applies it, he can envenom two ranged weapons or units of ammunition simultaneously with the same dose." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

trapsmith.name: Trapsmith
description: ## Trapsmith
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/135›

Some rogues are not content with just disabling traps - they love to build them, finding a captivating beauty in the turning of gears and the slither of ropes over pulleys. The trapsmith may have started out putting together traps in order to better understand how to disable them, but for most, it's long since gone beyond that - they now relish the challenge of creating the perfect combat machine.

::ab[Careful Disarm (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def ability="Whenever a trapsmith attempts to disarm a trap using Disable Device, she does not spring the trap unless she fails by 10 or more. If she does set off a trap she was attempting to disarm, she adds double her *trap sense* bonus to avoid the trap." replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Trap Master (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="Whenever a trapsmith disarms a trap using Disable Device, she can bypass it even if her check did not exceed the DC by 10 or more. If it is a magic trap that allows specific creatures to pass it without danger, she can modify which creatures it allows to pass, adding her allies and restricting enemies if she desires." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Befuddling strike~cunning trigger~quick disable~quick trapsmith"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Another day~frugal trapsmith~skill mastery"}

underground_chemist.name: Underground Chemist
description: ## Underground Chemist
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/109›

Underground chemists are part of the rotting, fetid underbelly of the alchemical world. While underground chemists can't hold a candle to dedicated alchemists, they're tricky and dangerous with alchemical substances and potions.

::ab[Chemical Weapons (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost ability="An underground chemist is able to retrieve an alchemical item as if ‹rule/drawing a weapon›." order=ap passive="She adds her Intelligence modifier to damage dealt with ‹rule/splash weapon«s›, including any splash damage. She adds 1/2 her level to Craft (alchemy) checks." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Precise Splash Weapons (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="An underground chemist can deal sneak attack damage with splash weapons. The attack must be her first attack that round, qualify for dealing ‹ability/sneak attack› damage (such as against a ‹rule/flat-footed› target), and be directed at a creature rather than a square." replace="Rogue talent gained at 4th level"}

::ab[Discovery (Su)]{l=10 icon=power ability="An underground chemist can select one of the following ‹ability/alchemist »discoveries› in place of a rogue talent: ‹discovery/concentrate poison›, ‹discovery/dilution›, ‹discovery/enhance potion›, ‹discovery/extend potion›, ‹discovery/mummification›, ‹discovery/nauseating flesh›, ‹discovery/poison conversion›, ‹discovery/preserve organs›, ‹discovery/spontaneous healing›, or ‹discovery/sticky poison›. She uses her rogue level as her alchemist level for determining the effects of her discoveries and whether she is able to select one." alter="Advanced talents"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Black market connections~fast fingers~lasting poison~sniper's eye~swift poison"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Deadly cocktail~thoughtful reexamining"}

vexing_dodger.name: Vexing Dodger
description: ## Vexing Dodger
‹SOURCE Giant Hunter's Handbook/21›
Vexing dodgers take advantage of their smaller size to outmaneuver larger foes such as giants. Vexing dodgers have the following class features.

::ab[Limb-Climber (Ex)]{icon=power-lower order=ap ability="When adjacent to or in the space of a corporeal creature at least one size category larger than herself, a vexing dodger can climb that creature's body with a successful ‹skill/Climb› check against a DC equal to the target creature's CMD. Although the vexing dodger is holding on to the creature, this action isn't a grapple; it doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity from the creature, and neither the vexing dodger nor the creature she climbs gains the grappled condition." passive="While the vexing dodger is on the climbed creature, the creature takes a penalty on attack rolls against the vexing dodger equal to the number of sneak attack dice the dodger possesses." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Improved Dirty Trick (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A vexing dodger gains ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." replace="Rogue talent gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Underfoot Agility (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A vexing dodger gains a morale bonus on Acrobatics, Climb, and Escape Artist attempts against creatures at least one size category larger than herself. @B-3f-rogue." replace="Trap sense"}

::ab[Underfoot Trickster (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A vexing dodger can move through the space of a creature two size categories larger than herself without needing to attempt an Acrobatics check. She would still need to succeed at Acrobatics checks to avoid attacks of opportunity provoked by leaving a threatened space; this ability covers only the ability to move." swift="Additionally, whenever the vexing dodger hits a larger foe with a melee sneak attack, she can choose to forgo the sneak attack bonus damage and instead attempt make a ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver." order=aw replace="Uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Distracting Climber (Ex)]{l=8 icon=boost ability="When a vexing dodger attempts a dirty trick maneuver against a creature she's climbing, she gets a bonus on the combat maneuver check equal to the number of sneak attack dice she has. If she chooses to ‹misc/sicken› the target with her dirty trick and succeeds at her combat maneuver check, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the vexing dodger's level + her Intelligence modifier) or be ‹misc/nauseated› for 1 round." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}

::ab[Rogue Talents (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A vexing dodger can choose ‹ability/uncanny dodge› as a rogue talent starting at 4th level, and she can choose ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› as an advanced rogue talent."}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{all="befuddling strike~confounding blades~peerless maneuver~positioning attack~slow reactions~wall scramble" link=talent}

waylayer.name: Waylayer
description: ## Waylayer
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/13›
A clever rogue knows the easiest battle to win is the one that's over before your foe knows it has begun. Some rogues thus specialize in setting up ambushes to catch their opponents off guard.

::ab[Staggering Reflexes (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A waylayer gains a bonus on initiative checks equal to 1/2 her level (minimum +1) if she acts in a surprise round, and is never ‹rule/flat-footed› at the start of combat (even if she fails to act during the surprise round)." replace=Trapfinding}

::ab[Ambuscading Sneak Attack (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost flavor="A waylayer becomes especially skilled at attacking unaware foes." ability="When she makes a sneak attack during a surprise round, she uses d8s to roll sneak attack damage instead of d6s." replace="Uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Danger Awareness (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power-def passive="A waylayer is never considered an unaware combatant and is always able to act in the surprise round." replace="Improved uncanny dodge"}
::ab[Exceptional Reflexes (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A waylayer can move up to her speed before taking her action in a surprise round." replace="Rogue talent gained at 12th level"}
::ab[Masterful Reflexes (Ex)]{l=20 icon=roll ability="A waylayer's initiative roll is automatically a natural 20." replace="Rogue talent gained at 20th level"}
::list[These rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Camouflage~cunning trigger~quick trapsmith~slow reactions~surprise attack~underhanded"}
::list[These advanced rogue talents complement this archetype]{link=talent hl all="Entanglement of blades~hide in plain sight~knock-out blow~weapon snatcher"}
